Hexagrams 57-64 were merged from the two single-lineage candidate files in data/sources/ — see AGENTS.md §7 and the status note below for the provenance and its caveats.
_meta.shape_note: Fields follow docs/DATA_MODEL.md. Loader confirmed to read this shape directly (extra fields like value/upper_trigram/all_lines_changing are ignored harmlessly). `lines` is an array of 6, bottom-to-top (position 1 = bottom). `all_lines_changing` is the special 用九/用六 text, used only when every line changes.

sources.chinese_original: Classical Zhouyi (周易) — ancient, public domain. Hexagrams 1-56 via ctext.org; 57-64 via zh.wikisource.org (from data/sources/hexagrams.1-64.legge.json).
sources.english: James Legge, 'The Yi King' (Sacred Books of the East, vol. 16). Public domain (Legge d. 1897). Hexagrams 1-56: 2nd ed. 1899, retrieved verbatim via the Chinese Text Project (ctext.org). Hexagrams 57-64: 1882 1st ed., read from an archive.org OCR scan (a DIFFERENT edition — wording may differ slightly from the 1899 house style; hex 64 line 6 was reassembled from two genuine OCR fragments and is the one field most worth re-verifying). Merged from data/sources/hexagrams.1-64.legge.json.
sources.vietnamese: Ngô Tất Tố, 'Kinh Dịch trọn bộ' — classic Vietnamese translation (Ngô Tất Tố d. 1954; public domain in Vietnam, life + 50 years p.m.a.). Extracted programmatically (matched against the verified Chinese text) from locally-supplied epub editions; 57-64 from data/sources/hexagrams.1-64.ngotatto.json. A small number of individual lines (see `vi: null` entries — including hex 58 judgment, hex 58 line 1, hex 59 line 2) had no separately extractable translation block in that source and are left null rather than guessed — never machine-invent vi text.

Hexagrams 57-64 were merged from the data/sources/ candidate files (after reading both files' _meta pros/cons per AGENTS.md §7); their line texts were normalized from a flat {position,zh,vi,en} shape into the nested {position,text:{...}} shape the loader expects. judgment.zh: 元亨,利貞。
judgment.vi: Kiền: Đầu cả, hanh thông, lợi tốt, chính bền.
judgment.en: Qian (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and firm.

image.zh: 天行健,君子以自強不息。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trời đi rnạnh, quân tử coi đó tự cường không nghỉ.
image.en: Heaven, in its motion, (gives the idea of) strength. The superior man, in accordance with this, nerves himself to ceaseless activity.

text.zh: 初九:潛龍,勿用。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Rồng lặn chớ dùng.
text.en: In the first (or lowest) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon lying hid (in the deep). It is not the time for active doing.

text.zh: 九二:見龍在田,利見大人。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai; Rồng hiện ở ruộng, lợi về sự thấy người lớn.
text.en: In the second NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon appearing in the field. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man.

text.zh: 九三:君子終日乾乾,夕惕若,厲,无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đấng quân tử trọn ngày săng sắc, tối dương rùng rợn. Nguy! Không lỗi.
text.en: In the third NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the superior man active and vigilant all the day, and in the evening still careful and apprehensive. (The position is) dangerous, but there will be no mistake.

text.zh: 九四:或躍在淵,无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Hoặc nhảy ở vực, không lỗi.
text.en: In the fourth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as the dragon looking) as if he were leaping up, but still in the deep. There will be no mistake.

text.zh: 九五:飛龍在天,利見大人。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Rồng bay ở trời, lợi về sự thấy người lớn.
text.en: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon on the wing in the sky. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man.

text.zh: 上九:亢龍有悔。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Rồng quá cực có ăn năn.
text.en: In the sixth (or topmost) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon exceeding the proper limits. There will be occasion for repentance.

all_lines_changing.vi: Hào Dùng Chín: Thấy đàn rồng không đầu, tốt.
all_lines_changing.en: (The lines of this hexagram are all strong and undivided, as appears from) the use of the number NINE. If the host of dragons (thus) appearing were to divest themselves of their heads, there would be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 元亨,利牝馬之貞。君子有攸往,先迷後得主,利西南得朋,東北喪朋。安貞,吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Khôn: Đầu cả, hanh thông, Lợi về nết trinh của ngựa cái. Quân tử có sự đi. Trước mê, sau được. Chủ về lợi. Phía Tây Nam được bạn, phía Đông Bắc mất bạn. Yên phận giữ nết trinh thì tốt.
judgment.en: Kun (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and having the firmness of a mare. When the superior man (here intended) has to make any movement, if he take the initiative, he will go astray; if he follow, he will find his (proper) lord. The advantageousness will be seen in his getting friends in the south-west, and losing friends in the north-east. If he rest in correctness and firmness, there will be good fortune.

image.zh: 地勢坤,君子以厚德載物。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Thế đất là quẻ Khôn, đấng quân tử coi đó mà dùng đức dày chở các vật.
image.en: The (capacity and sustaining) power of the earth is what is denoted by Kun. The superior man, in accordance with this, with his large virtue supports (men and) things.

text.zh: 初六:履霜,堅冰至。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Xéo sương, váng rắn tới.
text.en: In the first SIX, divided, (we see its subject) treading on hoarfrost. The strong ice will come (by and by).

text.zh: 六二:直,方,大,不習无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Thẳng, vuông, lớn, không phải tập, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, (shows the attribute of) being straight, square, and great. (Its operation), without repeated efforts, will be in every respect advantageous.

text.zh: 六三:含章可貞。或從王事,无成有終。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Ngậm văn vẻ, có thể chính; hoặc theo đuổi việc nhà vua, không cậy công? Thì được tốt lành về sau.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, (shows its subject) keeping his excellence under restraint, but firmly maintaining it. If he should have occasion to engage in the king's service, though he will not claim the success (for himself), he will bring affairs to a good issue.

text.zh: 六四:括囊;无咎,无譽。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Thắt túi, không lỗi, không khen.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, (shows the symbol of) a sack tied up. There will be no ground for blame or for praise.

text.zh: 六五:黃裳,元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Quần vàng, cả tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, (shows) the yellow lower garment. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 上六:龍戰于野,其血玄黃。
text.vi: Thượng Lục: Long chiến vu dã, kỳ huyết huyền hoàng.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, (shows) dragons fighting in the wild. Their blood is purple and yellow.

all_lines_changing.vi: Hào dùng Sáu: Lợi về vĩnh viễn chính đính.
all_lines_changing.en: (The lines of this hexagram are all weak and divided, as appears from) the use of the number SIX. If those (who are thus represented) be perpetually correct and firm, advantage will arise.

judgment.zh: 屯：元亨，利貞，勿用有攸往，利建侯。
judgment.vi: Truân đầu cả, hanh thông, lợi tốt, chính bền, chớ dùng có thửa đi, lợi về dựng tước hầu.
judgment.en: Zhun (indicates that in the case which it presupposes) there will be great progress and success, and the advantage will come from being correct and firm. (But) any movement in advance should not be (lightly) undertaken. There will be advantage in appointing feudal princes.

image.zh: 雲，雷，屯；君子以經綸。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Mây sấm là quẻ Truân, đấng quân tử coi đó mà sửa sang thiên hạ.
image.en: (The trigram representing) clouds and (that representing) thunder form Zhun. The superior man, in accordance with this, (adjusts his measures of government) as in sorting the threads of the warp and woof.

text.zh: 初九：磐桓；利居貞，利建侯。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Quanh co, lợi về ở chính bền, lợi về dựng tước hầu.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows the difficulty (its subject has) in advancing. It will be advantageous for him to abide correct and firm; advantageous (also) to be made a feudal ruler.

text.zh: 六二：屯如邅如，乘馬班如。匪寇婚媾，女子貞不字，十年乃字。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Dường quanh co vậy, đường cưỡi ngựa rẽ ra vậy. Chẳng phải giặc, là dâu gia đó. Con gái trinh tiết không đặt tên chữ, mười năm mới đặt tên chữ.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows (its subject) distressed and obliged to return; (even) the horses of her chariot (also) seem to be retreating. (But) not by a spoiler (is she assailed), but by one who seeks her to be his wife. The young lady maintains her firm correctness, and declines a union. After ten years she will be united, and have children.

text.zh: 六三：即鹿无虞，惟入于林中，君子幾不如舍，往吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Theo hươu không có ngu nhân, chỉ vào trong rừng. Đấng quân tử biết cơ, không bằng bỏ đi thì hối tiếc.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows one following the deer without (the guidance of) the forester, and only finding himself in the midst of the forest. The superior man, acquainted with the secret risks, thinks it better to give up the chase. If he went forward, he would regret it.

text.zh: 六四：乘馬班如，求婚媾，往吉，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Cưỡi ngựa dường rẽ ra vậy, tìm dâu gia, đi thì tốt, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows (its subject as a lady), the horses of whose chariot appear in retreat. She seeks, however, (the help of) him who seeks her to be his wife. Advance will be fortunate; all will turn out advantageously.

text.zh: 九五：屯其膏，小貞吉，大貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Gian truân thừa ơn huệ, nhỏ mà trinh thì lành, lớn mà trinh thì dữ.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the difficulties in the way of (its subject's) dispensing the rich favours that might be expected from him. With firmness and correctness there will be good fortune in small things; (even) with them in great things there will be evil.

text.zh: 上六：乘馬班如，泣血漣如。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Cưỡi ngựa dường rẽ ra vậy; khóc ra máu đầm đìa vậy.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows (its subject) with the horses of his chariot obliged to retreat, and weeping tears of blood in streams.

judgment.zh: 蒙：亨。匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我。初筮告，再三瀆，瀆則不告。利貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Mông hanh, chẳng phải ta tìm trẻ thơ, trẻ thơ tìm ta. Mới bói bảo, hai, ba lần nhàm, không bảo. Lợi về sự chính.
judgment.en: Meng (indicates that in the case which it presupposes) there will be progress and success. I do not (go and) seek the youthful and inexperienced, but he comes and seeks me. When he shows (the sincerity that marks) the first recourse to divination, I instruct him. If he apply a second and third time, that is troublesome; and I do not instruct the troublesome. There will be advantage in being firm and correct.

image.zh: 山下出泉，蒙；君子以果行育德。
image.vi: Lời tượng nói rằng: Dưới núi ra suối, là quẻ Mông, đấng quân tử coi đó mà quyết việc làm, nuôi lấy đức.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain, and beneath it that for a spring issuing forth form Meng. The superior man, in accordance with this, strives to be resolute in his conduct and nourishes his virtue.

text.zh: 初六：發蒙，利用刑人，用說桎梏，以往吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Mở mang trẻ thơ, lợi dùng về sự hình phạt người ta để thoát gông cùm, đi thì hối tiếc.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, (has respect to) the dispelling of ignorance. It will be advantageous to use punishment (for that purpose), and to remove the shackles (from the mind). But going on in that way (of punishment) will give occasion for regret.

text.zh: 九二：包蒙吉；納婦吉；子克家。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Bao dung trẻ thơ, tốt. Nạp vợ, tốt. Con trị được nhà.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) exercising forbearance with the ignorant, in which there will be good fortune; and admitting (even the goodness of) women, which will also be fortunate. (He may be described also as) a son able to (sustain the burden of) his family.

text.zh: 六三：勿用取女；見金夫，不有躬，无攸利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Chớ dùng lấy gái, thấy chồng vàng, không có mình, không thửa lợi.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, (seems to say) that one should not marry a woman whose emblem it might be, for that, when she sees a man of wealth, she will not keep her person from him, and in no wise will advantage come from her.

text.zh: 六四：困蒙，吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Khốn về tăm tối, hối tiếc.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, (shows its subject as if) bound in chains of ignorance. There will be occasion for regret.

text.zh: 六五：童蒙，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu năm: Trẻ thơ, tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject as a simple lad without experience. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：擊蒙；不利為寇，利禦寇。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Đánh kẻ tối tăm, không lợi cho sự làm giặc, lợi cho sự chống giặc.
text.en: In the topmost NINE, undivided, we see one smiting the ignorant (youth). But no advantage will come from doing him an injury. Advantage would come from warding off injury from him.

judgment.zh: 需：有孚，光亨，貞吉。利涉大川。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Nhu, có đức tin, sáng láng, hanh thông, chính bền, tốt. Lợi sang sông lớn.
judgment.en: Xu intimates that, with the sincerity which is declared in it, there will be brilliant success. With firmness there will be good fortune; and it will be advantageous to cross the great stream.

image.zh: 雲上於天，需；君子以飲食宴樂。
image.vi: Lời tượng nói rằng: mây lên trời, là quẻ Nhu, đấng quân tử coi đó mà ăn uống yên vụi.
image.en: (The trigram for) clouds ascending over that for the sky forms Xu. The superior man, in accordance with this, eats and drinks, feasts and enjoys himself (as if there were nothing else to employ him).

text.zh: 初九：需于郊。利用恆，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Đợi ở đồng, lợi về dùng lẽ hằng. Không có lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject waiting in the distant border. It will be well for him constantly to maintain (the purpose thus shown), in which case there will be no error.

text.zh: 九二：需于沙。小有言，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Đợi ở bãi cát, hơi có điều tiếng, sau chót tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject waiting on the sand (of the mountain stream). He will (suffer) the small (injury of) being spoken (against), but in the end there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：需于泥，致寇至。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đợi ở bùn, dắt gỉặc đến.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject in the mud (close by the stream). He thereby invites the approach of injury.

text.zh: 六四：需于血，出自穴。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đợi chưng máu, ra tự hang.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject waiting in (the place of) blood. But he will get out of the cavern.

text.zh: 九五：需于酒食，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Đợi chưng rượu cơm, chính tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject waiting amidst the appliances of a feast. Through his firmness and correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：入于穴，有不速之客三人來，敬之終吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Vào chung hang, có ba người khách không mời mà đến, kính trọng họ, sau chót tốt.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject entered into the cavern. (But) there are three guests coming, without being urged, (to his help). If he receive them respectfully, there will be good fortune in the end.

judgment.zh: 訟：有孚，窒。惕中吉。終凶。利見大人，不利涉大川。
judgment.vi: Kiện, có thật, bị lấp, phải sợ, vừa phải, tốt, theo đuổi đến chót, xấu; lợi về sự thấy người lớn, không lợi về sự sang sông lớn.
judgment.en: Song intimates how, though there is sincerity in one's contention, he will yet meet with opposition and obstruction; but if he cherish an apprehensive caution, there will be good fortune, while, if he must prosecute the contention to the (bitter) end, there will be evil. It will be advantageous to see the great man; it will not be advantageous to cross the great stream.

image.zh: 天與水違行，訟；君子以作事謀始。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trời với nước đi trái nhau, là quẻ Tụng. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà dấy việc mưu tính lúc đầu.
image.en: (The trigram representing) heaven and (that representing) water, moving away from each other, form Song. The superior man, in accordance with this, in the transaction of affairs takes good counsel about his first steps.

text.zh: 初六：不永所事，小有言，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Chẳng lâu dài về việc của mình, hơi có điều tiếng, sau tốt.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject not perpetuating the matter about which (the contention is). He will suffer the small (injury) of being spoken against, but the end will be fortunate.

text.zh: 九二：不克訟，歸而逋，其邑人三百戶，无眚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Không được kiện, về mà trốn người làng mình ba trăm hộ, không có tội lỗi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject unequal to the contention. If he retire and keep concealed (where) the inhabitants of his city are (only) three hundred families, he will fall into no mistake.

text.zh: 六三：食舊德，貞厲，終吉，或從王事，无成。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Ăn về đức cũ, chính bền, lo sợ sau tốt, hoặc theo việc vua, không thành.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject keeping in the old place assigned for his support, and firmly correct. Perilous as the position is, there will be good fortune in the end. Should he perchance engage in the king's business, he will not (claim the merit of) achievement.

text.zh: 九四：不克訟，復即命渝，安貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Không thể kiện, lại theo mệnh, đổi ra yên bề chính bền, tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject unequal to the contention. He returns to (the study of Heaven's) ordinances, changes (his wish to contend), and rests in being firm and correct. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：訟元吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Kiện cả tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject contending - and with great good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：或錫之鞶帶，終朝三褫之。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: hoặc cho chiếc dải lưng da, trọn buổi sáng, ba lần lột lại.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows how its subject may have the leathern belt conferred on him (by the sovereign), and thrice it shall be taken from him in a morning.

judgment.zh: 師：貞，丈人，吉无咎。
judgment.vi: Quân chính, bậc trượng nhân tốt, không lỗi.
judgment.en: Shi indicates how, in the case which it supposes, with firmness and correctness, and (a leader of) age and experience, there will be good fortune and no error.

image.zh: 地中有水，師；君子以容民畜眾。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trong đất có nước, là quẻ sư, đấng quân tử coi đó là dung dân, nuôi quân.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth and in the midst of it that representing water, form Shi. The superior man, in accordance with this, nourishes and educates the people, and collects (from among them) the multitudes (of the hosts).

text.zh: 初六：師出以律，否臧凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Quân ra bằng luật, không khéo thì hung.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows the host going forth according to the rules (for such a movement). If these be not good, there will be evil.

text.zh: 九二：在師中吉，无咎，王三錫命。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Ở trong quân, vừa phải thì tốt, không lỗi, nhà vua ba lần cho mệnh.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows (the leader) in the midst of the host. There will be good fortune and no error. The king has thrice conveyed to him the orders (of his favour).

text.zh: 六三：師或輿尸，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Quân hoặc khiêng thây, hung.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows how the host may, possibly, have many inefficient leaders. There will be evil.

text.zh: 六四：師左次，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Quân lùi đóng, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows the host in retreat. There is no error.

text.zh: 六五：田有禽，利執言，无咎。長子帥師，弟子輿尸，貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Ruộng có chim, lợi cho sự có lời để nói, không lỗi. Con cả đem quân, con em khiêng thây, chinh cũng hung.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows birds in the fields, which it will be advantageous to seize (and destroy). In that case there will be no error. If the oldest son leads the host, and younger men (idly occupy offices assigned to them), however firm and correct he may be, there will be evil.

text.zh: 上六：大君有命，開國承家，小人勿用。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Đấng đại quân có mệnh, mở nước vâng nhà, kẻ tiểu nhân chớ dùng.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows the great ruler delivering his charges, (appointing some) to be rulers of states, and others to undertake the headship of clans; but small men should not be employed (in such positions).

judgment.zh: 比：吉。原筮元永貞，无咎。不寧方來，後夫凶。
judgment.vi: Liền nhau tốt, truy nguyên việc bói, đầu cả, lâu dài, chính bền, không lỗi. [Có việc] chẳng yên mới lại, sau trễ trượng phu, hung.
judgment.en: Bi indicates that (under the conditions which it supposes) there is good fortune. But let (the principal party intended in it) re-examine himself, (as if) by divination, whether his virtue be great, unintermitting, and firm. If it be so, there will be no error. Those who have not rest will then come to him; and with those who are (too) late in coming it will be ill.

image.zh: 地上有水，比；先王以建萬國，親諸侯。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên đất có nước, là quẻ Tỷ, đấng tiên vương coi đó mà dựng muôn nước, thân chư hầu.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth, and over it (that representing) water, form Bi. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, established the various states and maintained an affectionate relation to their princes.

text.zh: 初六：有孚，比之，无咎。有孚盈缶，終來有它吉。
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject seeking by his sincerity to win the attachment of his object. There will be no error. Let (the breast) be full of sincerity as an earthenware vessel is of its contents, and it will in the end bring other advantages.

text.zh: 六二：比之自內，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Liền lại tự bên trong, chính và tốt.
text.en: In the second SIX, divided, we see the movement towards union and attachment proceeding from the inward (mind). With firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：比之匪人。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Liền với người không đáng liền.
text.en: In the third SIX, divided, we see its subject seeking for union with such as ought not to be associated with.

text.zh: 六四：外比之，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Liền lại với kẻ ngoài, chính tốt.
text.en: In the fourth SIX, divided, we see its subject seeking for union with the one beyond himself. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：顯比，王用三驅，失前禽。邑人不誡，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Rõ rệt liền lại, nhà vua dùng đuổi ba mặt, mất con chim ở phía trước người làng không bảo, tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, affords the most illustrious instance of seeking union and attachment. (We seem to see in it) the king urging his pursuit of the game (only) in three directions, and allowing the escape of all the animals before him, while the people of his towns do not warn one another (to prevent it). There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：比之无首，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Gần liền không đầu, hung.
text.en: In the topmost SIX, divided, we see one seeking union and attachment without having taken the first step (to such an end). There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 小畜：亨。密雲不雨，自我西郊。
judgment.vi: Chứa nhỏ hanh thông, mây dầy không mưa, tự cõi Tây ta.
judgment.en: Xiao Xu indicates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and success. (We see) dense clouds, but no rain coming from our borders in the west.

image.zh: 風行天上，小畜；君子以懿文德。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gió đi trên trời là quẻ Tiểu Súc, đấng quân tử coi đó mà làm cho tốt đức văn.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the sky, and that representing wind moving above it, form Xiao Xu. The superior man, in accordance with this, adorns the outward manifestation of his virtue.

text.zh: 初九：復自道，何其咎，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Trở lại với đạo lý, còn lỗi gì? Tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject returning and pursuing his own course. What mistake should he fall into? There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九二：牽復，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Giật trở lại, tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject, by the attraction (of the former line), returning (to the proper course). There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：輿說輻，夫妻反目。
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, suggests the idea of a carriage, the strap beneath which has been removed, or of a husband and wife looking on each other with averted eyes.

text.zh: 六四：有孚，血去惕出，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Có tin, máu đi, sợ ra, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject possessed of sincerity. The danger of bloodshed is thereby averted, and his (ground for) apprehension dismissed. There will be no mistake.

text.zh: 九五：有孚攣如，富以其鄰。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Có tin, dường co quẹo vậy, giàu vì láng giềng.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject possessed of sincerity, and drawing others to unite with him. Rich in resources, he employs his neighbours (in the same cause with himself).

text.zh: 上九：既雨既處，尚德載，婦貞厲。月幾望，君子征凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Đã mưa, đã ở, chuộng đức chở, đàn bà chính bền, nguy. Mặt trăng hầu đến tuần vọng, đấng quân tử đi thì hung.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows how the rain has fallen, and the (onward progress) is stayed - (so) must we value the full accumulation of the virtue (represented by the upper trigram). But a wife (exercising restraint), however firm and correct she may be, is in a position of peril, (and like) the moon approaching to the full. If the superior man prosecute his measures (in such circumstances), there will be evil.

judgment.zh: 履虎尾，不咥人，亨。
judgment.vi: Xéo đuôi cọp, không cắn người, hanh.
judgment.en: (Lu suggests the idea of) one treading on the tail of a tiger, which does not bite him. There will be progress and success.

image.zh: 上天下澤，履；君子以辨上下，安民志。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên trời dưới chằm, là quẻ Lý. Đấng quân vương coi đó mà phân biệt trên, dưới, định chí dân.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the sky above, and below it (that representing the waters of) a marsh, form Lu. The superior man, in accordance with this, discriminates between high and low, and gives settlement to the aims of the people.

text.zh: 初九：素履，往无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Xéo theo sự vốn có, đi, không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject treading his accustomed path. If he go forward, there will be no error.

text.zh: 九二：履道坦坦，幽人貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Xéo đường bằng phẳng, người uẩn chính bền thì tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject treading the path that is level and easy - a quiet and solitary man, to whom, if he be firm and correct, there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：眇能視，跛能履，履虎尾，咥人，凶。武人為于大君。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Chột biết trông, què biết xéo, xéo đuôi cọp, cắn người, hung. Kẻ vũ nhân làm đấng đại quân.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows a one-eyed man (who thinks he) can see; a lame man (who thinks he) can walk well; one who treads on the tail of a tiger and is bitten. (All this indicates) ill fortune. We have a (mere) bravo acting the part of a great ruler.

text.zh: 九四：履虎尾，愬愬，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Xéo đuôi cọp, nơm nớp, sau chót tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject treading on the tail of a tiger. He becomes full of apprehensive caution, and in the end there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：夬履，貞厲。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Quyết xéo, chính bền nguy.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the resolute tread of its subject. Though he be firm and correct, there will be peril.

text.zh: 上九：視履考祥，其旋元吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Coi sự xéo, xét điềm lành, thửa quanh cả tốt.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, tells us to look at (the whole course) that is trodden, and examine the presage which that gives. If it be complete and without failure, there will be great good fortune.

judgment.zh: 泰：小往大來，吉亨。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Thái, nhỏ đi, lớn lại, lành tốt, hanh thông.
judgment.en: In Tai (we see) the little gone and the great come. (It indicates that) there will be good fortune, with progress and success.

image.zh: 天地交，泰；后以財成天地之道，輔相萬物，以左右民。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trời đất giao nhau là quẻ Thái, ông vua coi mà sửa nên đạo của trời đất, giúp tập sự nên phải của trời đất, để đỡ đần dân.
image.en: (The trigrams for) heaven and earth in communication together form Tai. The (sage) sovereign, in harmony with this, fashions and completes (his regulations) after the courses of heaven and earth, and assists the application of the adaptations furnished by them, - in order to benefit the people.

text.zh: 初九：拔茅茹，以其彙，征吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Nhổ cụm cỏ tranh, lấy vầng nó, đi tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, suggests the idea of grass pulled up, and bringing with it other stalks with whose roots it is connected. Advance (on the part of its subject) will be fortunate.

text.zh: 九二：包荒，用馮河，不遐遺，朋亡，得尚于中行。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Bao dung sự hoang rậm, dùng để tay không lội sông, không sót việc xa; bè cánh mất, được sánh ở hàng giữa.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows one who can bear with the uncultivated, will cross the He without a boat, does not forget the distant, and has no (selfish) friendships. Thus does he prove himself acting in accordance with the course of the due Mean.

text.zh: 九三：无平不陂，无往不復，艱貞无咎。勿恤其孚，于食有福。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Không chỗ bằng phẳng nào không lồi lõm, không sự đi nào không trở lại. Khó nhọc, chính bền, chớ lo thừa sự tín, chưng việc ăn hưởng có phúc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows that, while there is no state of peace that is not liable to be disturbed, and no departure (of evil men) so that they shall not return, yet when one is firm and correct, as he realises the distresses that may arise, he will commit no error.

text.zh: 六四：翩翩，不富，以其鄰，不戒以孚。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Phơi phới, chẳng giàu lấy thửa láng giềng, chẳng răn lấy tin.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject fluttering (down) - not relying on his own rich resources, but calling in his neighbours. (They all come) not as having received warning, but in the sincerity (of their hearts).

text.zh: 六五：帝乙歸妹，以祉元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Vua Đế Ất gả em gái, để có phúc cả tốt.
text.en: The fifth six, divided, reminds us of (king) Di-yi's (rule about the) marriage of his younger sister. By such a course there is happiness and there will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：城復于隍，勿用師。自邑告命，貞吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Thành trở về rãnh, chớ dùng quân tự ấp bảo mệnh, chính bền thẹn tiếc.
text.en: The sixth six, divided, shows us the city wall returned into the moat. It is not the time to use the army. (The subject of the line) may, indeed, announce his orders to the people of his own city; but however correct and firm he may be, he will have cause for regret.

judgment.zh: 否之匪人，不利君子貞，大往小來。
judgment.vi: Bỉ đấy, chẳng phải đạo người. Chẳng lợi cho sự chính bền của đấng quân tử, lớn đi nhỏ lại.
judgment.en: In Pi there is the want of good understanding between the (different classes of) men, and its indication is unfavourable to the firm and correct course of the superior man. We see in it the great gone and the little come.

image.zh: 天地不交，否；君子以儉德辟難，不可榮以祿。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trời đất không giao nhau là quẻ Bĩ, đấng quân tử coi đó để dè đức, lánh nạn, không thể vẻ vang bằng lộc.
image.en: (The trigrams of) heaven and earth, not in intercommunication, form Pi. The superior man, in accordance with this, restrains (the manifestation of) his virtue, and avoids the calamities (that threaten him). There is no opportunity of conferring on him the glory of emolument.

text.zh: 初六：拔茅茹，以其彙，貞吉亨。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Nhổ cụm cỏ tranh, lấy vầng nó, chính bền tốt lành hanh thông.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, suggests the idea of grass pulled up, and bringing with it other stalks with whose roots it is connected. With firm correctness (on the part of its subject), there will be good fortune and progress.

text.zh: 六二：包承。小人吉，大人否，亨。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Bọc chứa sự vâng thuận, kẻ tiểu nhân tốt, đấng đại nhân bĩ thì hanh thông.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject patient and obedient. To the small man (comporting himself so) there will be good fortune. If the great man (comport himself) as the distress and obstruction require, he will have success.

text.zh: 六三：包羞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Bọc chứa sự hổ thẹn.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject ashamed of the purpose folded (in his breast).

text.zh: 九四：有命，无咎，疇離祉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Có mệnh không lỗi, bè loại dính phúc.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject acting in accordance with the ordination (of Heaven), and committing no error. His companions will come and share in his happiness.

text.zh: 九五：休否，大人吉。其亡其亡，繫于苞桑。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Nghỉ bĩ, đấng đại nhân tốt. Này mất! Này mất! Buộc cây dâu rậm.
text.en: In the fifth NINE, undivided, we see him who brings the distress and obstruction to a close,--the great man and fortunate. (But let him say), 'We may perish! We may perish!' (so shall the state of things become firm, as if) bound to a clump of bushy mulberry trees.

text.zh: 上九：傾否，先否後喜。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Nghiêng bĩ, trước bĩ sau mừng.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows the overthrow (and removal of) the condition of distress and obstruction. Before this there was that condition. Hereafter there will be joy.

judgment.zh: 同人于野，亨。利涉大川，利君子貞。
judgment.vi: Cùng người ở đồng, hanh thông, lợi về sự sang sông lớn, lợi cho sự chính bền của đấng quân tử.
judgment.en: Tong Ren (or 'Union of men') appears here (as we find it) in the (remote districts of the) country, indicating progress and success. It will be advantageous to cross the great stream. It will be advantageous to maintain the firm correctness of the superior man.

image.zh: 天與火，同人；君子以類族辨物。
image.vi: Lời tượng nói rằng: Trời với lửa là quẻ Đồng Nhân, đấng quân tử coi đó mà chia loại các giống, phân biệt các vật.
image.en: (The trigrams for) heaven and fire form Tong Ren. The superior man, in accordance with this), distinguishes things according to their kinds and classes.

text.zh: 初九：同人于門，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Cùng người ở cửa, không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men just issuing from his gate. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：同人于宗，吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Cùng người ở họ, đáng tiếc.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in relation with his kindred. There will be occasion for regret.

text.zh: 九三：伏戎于莽，升其高陵，三歲不興。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Phục quân ở rừng, lên thửa gò cao, ba năm không dấy lên.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) with his arms hidden in the thick grass, and at the top of a high mound. (But) for three years he makes no demonstration.

text.zh: 九四：乘其墉，弗克攻，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Cưỡi thừa tường, không đánh được, tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) mounted on the city wall; but he does not proceed to make the attack (he contemplates). There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：同人，先號咷而後笑。大師克相遇。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Cùng với người, trước kêu gào mà sau cười, quân lớn được, gặp nhau.
text.en: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (the representative of) the union of men first wails and cries out, and then laughs. His great host conquers, and he (and the subject of the second line) meet together.

text.zh: 上九：同人于郊，无悔。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Cùng người ở đồng, không ăn năn.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in the suburbs. There will be no occasion for repentance.

judgment.zh: 大有：元亨。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Đại Hữu cả lớn hanh thông.
judgment.en: Da You indicates that, (under the circumstances which it implies), there will be great progress and success.

image.zh: 火在天上，大有；君子以遏惡揚善，順天休命。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Lửa cao ở trên trời, là quẻ Đại Hữu, đấng quân tử coi đó mà ngăn kẻ ác, biểu dương người thiện, thuận theo mệnh tốt của trời.
image.en: (The trigram for) heaven and (that of) fire above it form Da You. The superior man, in accordance with this, represses what is evil and gives distinction to what is good, in sympathy with the excellent Heaven-conferred (nature).

text.zh: 初九：无交害，匪咎，艱則无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Không dính tới sự hại, chẳng phải lỗi. Khó nhọc thì không lỗi.
text.en: There is no approach to what is injurious, and there is no error. Let there be a realisation of the difficulty (and danger of the position), and there will be no error (to the end).

text.zh: 九二：大車以載，有攸往，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Xe lớn để chở, có thừa đi, không lỗi.
text.en: A large waggon with its load. In whatever direction advance is made, there will be no error.

text.zh: 九三：公用亨于天子，小人弗克。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba. Tước công dùng hưởng của đấng thiên tử, kẻ tiểu nhân không thể được.
text.en: A feudal prince presenting his offerings to the Son of Heaven. A small man would be unequal (to such a duty).

text.zh: 九四：匪其彭，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Chẳng phải sự thịnh của mình, không lỗi.
text.en: Its subject keeping his great resources under restraint. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六五：厥孚交如，威如；吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Thửa tin dường giao nhau vậy, dường oai nghiêm vậy, tốt.
text.en: The sincerity of its subject reciprocated by that of all the others. Let him display a proper majesty, and there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：自天祐之，吉无不利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Tự trời giúp nó, tốt, không gì không lợi.
text.en: Its subject with help accorded to him from Heaven. There will be good fortune, advantage in every respect.

judgment.zh: 謙：亨，君子有終。
judgment.en: Qian indicates progress and success. The superior man, (being humble as it implies), will have a (good) issue (to his undertakings).

image.zh: 地中有山，謙；君子以裒多益寡，稱物平施。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trong đất có núi, là quẻ Khiêm, đấng quân tử coi đó mà rút chỗ nhiều, thêm chỗ ít, cân lường các vật, làm cho sự thi thố được thăng bằng.
image.en: (The trigram for) the earth and (that of) a mountain in the midst of it form Qian. The superior man, in accordance with this, diminishes what is excessive (in himself), and increases where there is any defect, bringing about an equality, according to the nature of the case, in his treatment (of himself and others).

text.zh: 初六：謙謙君子，用涉大川，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Đấng quân tử nhún nhún, dùng sang sông lớn, tốt.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows us the superior man who adds humility to humility. (Even) the great stream may be crossed with this, and there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六二：鳴謙，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Kêu sự nhún, chính bền, tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows us humility that has made itself recognised. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：勞謙，君子有終，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba; Đấng quân tử nhọc mà nhún, sau chót, tốt.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows the superior man of (acknowledged) merit. He will maintain his success to the end, and have good fortune.

text.zh: 六四：无不利，撝謙。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Không gì không lợi, vung vẩy sự nhún.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows one, whose action would be in every way advantageous, stirring up (the more) his humility.

text.zh: 六五：不富，以其鄰，利用侵伐，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Không giàu, sai khiến được láng giềng, lợi dụng lấn đánh, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows one who, without being rich, is able to employ his neighbours. He may advantageously use the force of arms. All his movements will be advantageous.

text.zh: 上六：鳴謙，利用行師，征邑國。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Kêu sự nhún, lợi dụng trẩy quân đánh làng nước.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows us humility that has made itself recognised. The subject of it will with advantage put his hosts in motion; but (he will only) punish his own towns and state.

judgment.zh: 豫：利建侯，行師。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Dự, lợi cho sự dựng nước hầu, trẩy quân.
judgment.en: Yu indicates that, (in the state which it implies), feudal princes may be set up, and the hosts put in motion, with advantage.

image.zh: 雷出地奮，豫；先王以作樂崇德，殷薦之上帝，以配祖考。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng; Sấm ra, đất chuyển, là quẻ Dự, đấng tiên vương coi đó mà làm nhạc, chuộng đức, thịnh dâng đức Thượng đế để phối ông cha.
image.en: (The trigrams for) the earth and thunder issuing from it with its crashing noise form Yu. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, composed their music and did honour to virtue, presenting it especially and most grandly to God, when they associated with Him (at the service) their highest ancestor and their father.

text.zh: 初六：鳴豫，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Kêu sự vui, hung!
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject proclaiming his pleasure and satisfaction. There will be evil.

text.zh: 六二：介于石，不終日，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Tiết tháo như đá, không trọn ngày, chính bền thì tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one who is firm as a rock. (He sees a thing) without waiting till it has come to pass; with his firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：盱豫，悔。遲有悔。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Nhìn sự vui, ăn năn chậm, có ăn năn.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows one looking up (for favours), while he indulges the feeling of pleasure and satisfaction. If he would understand!--If he be late in doing so, there will indeed be occasion for repentance.

text.zh: 九四：由豫，大有得。勿疑。朋盍簪。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Bởi đó là vui, cả có được, chớ nghi ngờ, bè bạn tụ họp.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows him from whom the harmony and satisfaction come. Great is the success which he obtains. Let him not allow suspicions to enter his mind, and thus friends will gather around him.

text.zh: 六五：貞疾，恆不死。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Chính bền có tật, thường không chết.
text.en: The fifth six, divided, shows one with a chronic complaint, but who lives on without dying.

text.zh: 上六：冥豫，成有渝，无咎。
text.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Mờ tối về sự vui, việc thành rồi, có thay đổi, không lỗi.
text.en: The topmost six, divided, shows its subject with darkened mind devoted to the pleasure and satisfaction (of the time); but if he change his course even when (it may be considered as) completed, there will be no error.

judgment.zh: 隨：元亨利貞，无咎。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tùy cả, hanh, lợi, trinh, không lỗi.
judgment.en: Sui indicates that (under its conditions) there will be great progress and success. But it will be advantageous to be firm and correct. There will (then) be no error.

image.zh: 澤中有雷，隨；君子以嚮晦入宴息。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trong chằm có sấm, là quẻ Tùy, đấng quân tử coi đó mà sắp tối vào nghỉ ngơi.
image.en: (The trigram for the waters of) a marsh and (that for) thunder (hidden) in the midst of it form Sui. The superior man in accordance with this, when it is getting towards dark, enters (his house) and rests.

text.zh: 初九：官有渝，貞吉。出門交有功。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Quan có thay đổi, chính thì tốt, ra cửa kết bạn thì có công.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows us one changing the object of his pursuit; but if he be firm and correct, there will he good fortune. Going beyond (his own) gate to find associates, he will achieve merit.

text.zh: 六二：系小子，失丈夫。
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows us one who cleaves to the little boy, and lets go the man of age and experience.

text.zh: 六三：系丈夫，失小子。隨有求得，利居貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Quấn quít đấng trượng phu, mất kẻ tiểu tử, theo mà có tìm, lợi về ở trinh.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows us one who cleaves to the man of age and experience, and lets go the little boy. Such following will get what it seeks; but it will be advantageous to adhere to what is firm and correct.

text.zh: 九四：隨有獲，貞凶。有孚在道，以明，何咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín tư: Theo có được, chính mà hung; có tin ở đạo, dùng sáng, lỗi gì?
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows us one followed and obtaining (adherents). Though he be firm and correct, there will be evil. If he be sincere (however) in his course, and make that evident, into what error will he fall?

text.zh: 九五：孚于嘉，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Tin về kẻ lành, tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows us (the ruler) sincere in (fostering all) that is excellent. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：拘系之，乃從維之。王用亨于西山。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Cầm buộc đó, bèn theo ràng đó, vua dùng hưởng ở non Tây.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows us (that sincerity) firmly held and clung to, yea, and bound fast. (We see) the king with it presenting his offerings on the western mountain.

judgment.zh: 蠱：元亨，利涉大川。先甲三日，後甲三日。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Cổ cả, hanh, lợi về sang sông lớn. Trước Giáp ba ngày, sau Giáp ba ngày.
judgment.en: Gu indicates great progress and success (to him who deals properly with the condition represented by it). There will be advantage in (efforts like that of) crossing the great stream.

image.zh: 山下有風，蠱；君子以振民育德。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới núi có gió, là quẻ Cổ, đấng quân tử coi đó mà chấn dân nuôi đức.
image.en: (The trigram for) a mountain, and below it that for wind, form Gu. The superior man, in accordance with this, (addresses himself to) help the people and nourish his own virtue.

text.zh: 初六：幹父之蠱，有子，考无咎，厲終吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Cán đáng cuộc Cổ của cha, có con, không lỗi, nguy, sau chót tốt.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows (a son) dealing with the troubles caused by his father. If he be an (able) son, the father will escape the blame of having erred. The position is perilous, but there will be good fortune in the end.

text.zh: 九二：幹母之蠱，不可貞。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Cán đáng sự Cổ của mẹ, không thể trinh.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows (a son) dealing with the troubles caused by his mother. He should not (carry) his firm correctness (to the utmost).

text.zh: 九三：幹父之蠱，小有悔，无大咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Cán đáng sự Cổ của cha, hơi có ăn năn, không có lỗi lớn.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows (a son) dealing with the troubles caused by his father. There may be some small occasion for repentance, but there will not be any great error.

text.zh: 六四：裕父之蠱，往見吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Rộng rãi với sự Cổ của cha, đi thấy tiếc.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows (a son) viewing indulgently the troubles caused by his father. If he go forward, he will find cause to regret it.

text.zh: 六五：幹父之蠱，用譽。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Cán đáng sự Cổ của cha, dùng khen.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows (a son) dealing with the troubles caused by his father. He obtains the praise of using (the fit instrument for his work).

text.zh: 上九：不事王侯，高尚其事。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Chẳng thờ tước vương tước hầu, cao nâng thửa việc.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows us one who does not serve either king or feudal lord, but in a lofty spirit prefers (to attend to) his own affairs.

judgment.zh: 臨：元，亨，利，貞。至于八月有凶。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Lâm, cả, hanh, lợi, trinh, đến chưng tám tháng sau, có hung.
judgment.en: Lin (indicates that under the conditions supposed in it) there will be great progress and success, while it will be advantageous to be firmly correct. In the eighth month there will be evil.

image.zh: 澤上有地，臨；君子以教思无窮，容保民无疆。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên chằm có đất là quẻ Lâm, đấng quân tử coi đó mà tứ dậy không cùng, dong giữ dân không bờ.
image.en: (The trigram for) the waters of a marsh and that for the earth above it form Lin. The superior man, in accordance with this, has his purposes of instruction that are inexhaustible, and nourishes and supports the people without limit.

text.zh: 初九：咸臨，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Đều tới, chính tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject advancing in company (with the subject of the second line). Through his firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九二：咸臨，吉无不利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Đều tới, tốt, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject advancing in company (with the subject of the first line). There will be good fortune; (advancing) will be in every way advantageous.

text.zh: 六三：甘臨，无攸利。既憂之，无咎。
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows one well pleased (indeed) to advance, (but whose action) will be in no way advantageous. If he become anxious about it (however), there will be no error.

text.zh: 六四：至臨，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư; Rất tới, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows one advancing in the highest mode. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六五：知臨，大君之宜，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Khôn tới, sự nên của vua cả, tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the advance of wisdom, such as befits the great ruler. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：敦臨，吉无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Dầy tới, tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows the advance of honesty and generosity. There will be good fortune, and no error.

judgment.zh: 觀：盥而不薦，有孚顒若。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Quán, rửa mà không cứng, có tin, dường cung kính vậy.
judgment.en: Guan shows (how he whom it represents should be like) the worshipper who has washed his hands, but not (yet) presented his offerings - with sincerity and an appearance of dignity (commanding reverent regard).

image.zh: 風行地上，觀；先王以省方觀民設教。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gió đi trên đất là quẻ Quán, đấng tiên vương coi đó mà xét các phương, xem tục dân, đặt sự dạy.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth, and that for wind moving above it, form Guan. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, examined the (different) regions (of the kingdom), to see the (ways of the) people, and set forth their instructions.

text.zh: 初六：童觀，小人无咎，君子吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Trẻ xem, kẻ tiểu nhân không lỗi, đấng quân tử đáng tiếc.
text.en: The looking of a lad - not blamable in men of inferior rank, but matter for regret in superior men.

text.zh: 六二：闚觀，利女貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Nhòm xem, lợi về sự trinh của con gái.
text.en: One peeping out from a door. It would be advantageous if it were (merely) the firm correctness of a female.

text.zh: 六三：觀我生，進退。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Xem ta sinh, tiến lui.
text.en: One looking at (the course of) his own life, to advance or recede (accordingly).

text.zh: 六四：觀國之光，利用賓于王。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Xem sự sáng láng của nước, lợi dụng làm khách chưng vua.
text.en: One contemplating the glory of the kingdom. It will be advantageous for him, being such as he is, (to seek) to be a guest of the king.

text.zh: 九五：觀我生，君子无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Xem ta sinh, quân tử, không lỗi.
text.en: Its subject contemplating his own life(-course). A superior man, he will (thus) fall into no error.

text.zh: 上九：觀其生，君子无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Xem thửa sinh, quân tử không lỗi.
text.en: Its subject contemplating his character to see if it be indeed that of a superior man. He will not fall into error.

judgment.zh: 噬嗑：亨。利用獄。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Phệ Hạp hanh, lợi dùng việc ngục.
judgment.en: Shi He indicates successful progress (in the condition of things which it supposes). It will be advantageous to use legal constraints.

image.zh: 雷電，噬嗑；先王以明罰敕法。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sấm chớp là quẻ Phệ Hạp, đấng tiên vương coi đó để tỏ phạt truyền phép.
image.en: (The trigrams representing) thunder and lightning form Shi He. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, framed their penalties with intelligence, and promulgated their laws.

text.zh: 初九：屨校滅趾，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu; Xéo xiềng, đứt ngón chân, không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows one with his feet in the stocks and deprived of his toes. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：噬膚滅鼻，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Cắn da, dứt mũi, không lỗi.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one biting through the soft flesh, and (going on to) bite off the nose. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六三：噬臘肉，遇毒；小吝，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: cắn mắm khô, gặp độc, hơi tiếc, không lỗi.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows one gnawing dried flesh, and meeting with what is disagreeable. There will be occasion for some small regret, but no (great) error.

text.zh: 九四：噬乾胏，得金矢，利艱貞，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Cắn chạo khô, được tên vàng, lợi về khó nhọc, chính bền tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows one gnawing the flesh dried on the bone, and getting the pledges of money and arrows. It will be advantageous to him to realise the difficulty of his task and be firm,--in which case there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六五：噬乾肉，得黃金，貞厲，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Cắn thịt khô, được vàng sắc vàng, chính bền, lo sợ, không lỗi.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows one gnawing at dried flesh, and finding the yellow gold. Let him be firm and correct, realising the peril (of his position). There will be no error.

text.zh: 上九：何校滅耳，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín trên: Đội xiềng, đứt tai, hung.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows one wearing the cangue, and deprived of his ears. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 賁：亨。小利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Bí hanh, hơi lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Bi indicates that there should be free course (in what it denotes). There will be little advantage (however) if it be allowed to advance (and take the lead).

image.zh: 山下有火，賁；君子以明庶政，无敢折獄。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới núi có lửa là quẻ Bí, đấng quản tử coi đó mà tỏ mọi chính, không quả cảm về việc đoán ngục.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and that for fire under it form Bi. The superior man, in accordance with this, throws a brilliancy around his various processes of government, but does not dare (in a similar way) to decide cases of criminal litigation.

text.zh: 初九：賁其趾，舍車而徒。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Trang sức thửa ngón chân, bỏ xe mà đi không.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows one adorning (the way of) his feet. He can discard a carriage and walk on foot.

text.zh: 六二：賁其須。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Trang sức cái râu của mình.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one adorning his beard.

text.zh: 九三：賁如濡如，永貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Rõ ràng vậy, bóng mượt vậy; mãi mãi chính bền, tốt.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject with the appearance of being adorned and bedewed (with rich favours). But let him ever maintain his firm correctness, and there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六四：賁如皤如，白馬翰如，匪寇婚媾。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Rỡ ràng vậy, phơ phơ vậy, ngựa trắng có cánh vậy. Chẳng phải giặc, là dâu gia.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows one looking as if adorned, but only in white. (It shows also) a white horse (whose pace seems to be that) of a bird flying. (The subject of it) is not a robber, but is seeking (the subject of the first line) to be his wife.

text.zh: 六五：賁于丘園，束帛戔戔，吝，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Trang sức ở gò vườn, bó lụa mỏng hẹp, đáng tiếc, sau chót tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject adorned, as in a hill garden, where the bundles of silk (placed as offerings) are small and mean. It will be matter for regret, but there will be good fortune in the end.

text.zh: 上九：白賁，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Trang sức bằng màu trắng, không lỗi.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows one with white as his (only) ornament. There will be no error.

judgment.zh: 剝：不利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Bác không lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Bo indicates that (in the state which it symbolises) it will not be advantageous to make a movement in any direction whatever.

image.zh: 山附於地，剝；上以厚下安宅。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Núi phụ ở đất, là quẻ Bác, người trên coi đó mà hậu cho kẻ dưới, làm yên chỗ ở.
image.en: (The trigrams representing) the earth, and (above it) that for a mountain, which adheres to the earth, form Bo. Superiors, in accordance with this, seek to strengthen those below them, to secure the peace and stability of their own position.

text.zh: 初六：剝床以足，蔑貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Đẽo giường bằng chân, không trinh, hung.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows one overturning the couch by injuring its legs. (The injury will go on to) the destruction of (all) firm correctness, and there will be evil.

text.zh: 六二：剝床以辨，蔑貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Đẽo giường bằng bễ, chẳng trinh, hung.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one overthrowing the couch by injuring its frame. (The injury will go on to) the destruction of (all) firm correctness, and there will be evil.

text.zh: 六三：剝之，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Đẽo đó, không lỗi.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject among the overthrowers; but there will be no error.

text.zh: 六四：剝床以膚，凶。
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject having overthrown the couch, and (going to injure) the skin (of him who lies on it). There will be evil.

text.zh: 六五：貫魚，以宮人寵，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Xâu cá; lấy cung nhân được yên, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows (its subject leading on the others like) a string of fishes, and (obtaining for them) the favour that lights on the inmates of the palace. There will be advantage in every way.

text.zh: 上九：碩果不食，君子得輿，小人剝廬。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Trái lớn không ăn, đấng quân tử được xe, kẻ tiểu nhân đẽo nhà.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) a great fruit which has not been eaten. The superior man finds (the people again) as a chariot carrying him. The small men (by their course) overthrow their own dwellings.

judgment.zh: 復：亨。出入无疾，朋來无咎。反復其道，七日來復，利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Phục hanh, ra vào không tật, bạn đến không lỗi. Lật đi lật lại thửa đạo, bảy ngày lại trở lại, lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Fu indicates that there will be free course and progress (in what it denotes). (The subject of it) finds no one to distress him in his exits and entrances; friends come to him, and no error is committed. He will return and repeat his (proper) course. In seven days comes his return. There will be advantage in whatever direction movement is made.

image.zh: 雷在地中，復；先王以至日閉關，商旅不行，后不省方。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sấm ở trong đất, là quẻ Phục, đấng tiên vương coi đó mà ngày chí đóng cửa ải, kẻ thương lữ không đi, các vua không xét các phương.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth and that for thunder in the midst of it form Fu. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, on the day of the (winter) solstice, shut the gates of the passes (from one state to another), so that the travelling merchants could not (then) pursue their journeys, nor the princes go on with the inspection of their states.

text.zh: 初九：不遠復，无祗悔，元吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Chẳng xa trở lại, không đến ăn năn, cả tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject returning (from an error) of no great extent, which would not proceed to anything requiring repentance. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 六二：休復，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Đẹp sự trở lại, tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows the admirable return (of its subject). There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：頻復，厲无咎。
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows one who has made repeated returns. The position is perilous, but there will be no error.

text.zh: 六四：中行獨復。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đi giữa một mình trở lại.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject moving right in the centre (among those represented by the other divided lines), and yet returning alone (to his proper path).

text.zh: 六五：敦復，无悔。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Dốc lòng về sự trở lại, không phải ăn năn.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the noble return of its subject. There will be no ground for repentance.

text.zh: 上六：迷復，凶，有災眚。用行師，終有大敗，以其國君，凶；至于十年，不克征。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Lú lấp sự trở lại, hung. Có vạ tội, dùng để trẩy quân, sau chót có thua lớn; tới cả vua nước đó cũng hung, đến chừng mười năm, không thể đi.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject all astray on the subject of returning. There will be evil. There will be calamities and errors. If with his views he put the hosts in motion, the end will be a great defeat, whose issues will extend to the ruler of the state. Even in ten years he will not be able to repair the disaster.

judgment.zh: 无妄：元亨，利貞。其匪正有眚，不利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Vô Vọng cả hanh, lợi về sự chính bền; thửa chẳng chính có tội, không lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Wu Wang indicates great progress and success, while there will be advantage in being firm and correct. If (its subject and his action) be not correct, he will fall into errors, and it will not be advantageous for him to move in any direction.

image.zh: 天下雷行，物與无妄；先王以茂對時育萬物。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới trời sấm chạy vật được phú tính, là quẻ Vô Vọng; đấng tiên vương coi đó mà tốt việc đối thì, nuôi muôn vật.
image.en: The thunder rolls all under the sky, and to (every)thing there is given (its nature), free from all insincerity. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, (made their regulations) in complete accordance with the seasons, thereby nourishing all things.

text.zh: 初九：无妄，往吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Không càn, đi tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject free from all insincerity. His advance will be accompanied with good fortune.

text.zh: 六二：不耕獲，不菑畬，則利有攸往。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Chẳng cày, gặt; chẳng ngả, ngấu thì lợi có thửa đi.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one who reaps without having ploughed (that he might reap), and gathers the produce of his third year's fields without having cultivated them the first year for that end. To such a one there will be advantage in whatever direction he may move.

text.zh: 六三：无妄之災，或繫之牛，行人之得，邑人之災。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Cái hại của sự không càn, hoặc buộc con trâu, kẻ đi đường mà được, người ấp bị hại.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows calamity happening to one who is free from insincerity - as in the case of an ox that has been tied up. A passer by finds it (and carries it off), while the people in the neighbourhood have the calamity (of being accused and apprehended).

text.zh: 九四：可貞，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Khả trinh, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows (a case) in which, if its subject can remain firm and correct, there will be no error.

text.zh: 九五：无妄之疾，勿藥有喜。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Cái tật không càn, đừng thuốc, có mừng.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows one who is free from insincerity, and yet has fallen ill. Let him not use medicine, and he will have occasion for joy (in his recovery).

text.zh: 上九：无妄，行有眚，无攸利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Không càn mà đi, có tội, không thửa lợi.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject free from insincerity, yet sure to fall into error, if he take action. (His action) will not be advantageous in any way.

judgment.zh: 大畜：利貞，不家食吉，利涉大川。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Đại Súc lợi về sự chính, chẳng ăn ở nhà, tốt lợi sang sông lớn.
judgment.en: Under the conditions of Da Xu it will be advantageous to be firm and correct. (If its subject do not seek to) enjoy his revenues in his own family (without taking service at court), there will be good fortune. It will be advantageous for him to cross the great stream.

image.zh: 天在山中，大畜；君子以多識前言往行，以畜其德。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trời ở trong núi, là quẻ Đại Súc, đấng quân tử coi đó mà ghi nhiều lời trước, nết xưa, để nuôi đức mình.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain, and in the midst of it that (representing) heaven, form Da Xu. The superior man, in accordance with this, stores largely in his memory the words and deeds of former men, to subserve the accumulation of his virtue.

text.zh: 初九：有厲利已。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Có nguy, lợi thôi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject in a position of peril. It will be advantageous for him to stop his advance.

text.zh: 九二：輿說輹。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Xe trút bánh.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows a carriage with the strap under it removed.

text.zh: 九三：良馬逐，利艱貞。曰閑輿衛，利有攸往。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Ngựa hay ruổi, ngày quen xe, lại có thửa đi.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject urging his way with good horses. It will be advantageous for him to realise the difficulty (of his course), and to be firm and correct, daily practising himself in his charioteering and methods of defence. (Thus) advantage will come to him in whatever direction he may move.

text.zh: 六四：童牛之牿，元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Cái cùm trâu non, cả tốt.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows the young bull, (and yet) having the piece of wood over his horns. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 六五：豶豕之牙，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Nanh con lợn thiến, tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the teeth of a castrated hog. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：何天之衢，亨。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Sao đường trời hanh.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) in command of the firmament of heaven. There will be progress.

judgment.zh: 頤：貞吉。觀頤，自求口實。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Di chính tốt, xem sự nuôi, tự tìm cái thật của miệng.
judgment.en: Yi indicates that with firm correctness there will be good fortune (in what is denoted by it). We must look at what we are seeking to nourish, and by the exercise of our thoughts seek for the proper aliment.

image.zh: 山下有雷，頤；君子以慎言語，節飲食。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới núi có sấm, là quẻ Di, đấng quân tử coi đó mà cẩn thận nói năng, dè dặt uống ăn.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and under it that for thunder form Yi. The superior man, in accordance with this, (enjoins) watchfulness over our words, and the temperate regulation of our eating and drinking.

text.zh: 初九：舍爾靈龜，觀我朵頤，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Bỏ con rùa thiêng của mày, xem ta trễ mép, hung.
text.en: 'You leave your efficacious tortoise, and look at me till your lower jaw hangs down.' There will be evil.

text.zh: 六二：顛頤，拂經，于丘頤，征凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáụ Hai: Đảo nuôi, trái thường ở gò, đi thì hung.
text.en: One looking downwards for nourishment, which is contrary to what is proper; or seeking it from the height (above), advance towards which will lead to evil.

text.zh: 六三：拂頤，貞凶，十年勿用，无攸利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Trái nuôi, chính hung, mười năm chớ dùng, không thửa lợi.
text.en: One acting contrary to the method of nourishing. However firm he may be, there will be evil. For ten years let him not take any action, (for) it will not be in any way advantageous.

text.zh: 六四：顛頤，吉，虎視眈眈，其欲逐逐，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đảo nuôi, tốt, hổ trông hau háu, lòng muốn của nó liền liền, không lỗi.
text.en: One looking downwards for (the power to) nourish. There will be good fortune. (Such a one is like) a tiger glaring and watching with impatient eagerness; (but) he will fall into no error.

text.zh: 六五：拂經，居貞吉，不可涉大川。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Trái thường, ở chính tốt, chăng khá sang sông lớn.
text.en: One acting contrary to what is regular and proper; but if he abide in firmness, there will be good fortune. He should not, (however, try to) cross the great stream.

text.zh: 上九：由頤，厲吉，利涉大川。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Bởi nuôi, nguy, tốt, lợi sang sông lớn.
text.en: Him from whom comes the nourishing. His position is perilous, but there will be good fortune. It will be advantageous to cross the great stream.

judgment.zh: 大過：棟橈，利有攸往，亨。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Đại Quá, cột ỏe, lợi có thửa đi, hanh.
judgment.en: Da Guo suggests to us a beam that is weak. There will be advantage in moving (under its conditions) in any direction whatever; there will be success.

image.zh: 澤滅木，大過；君子以獨立不懼，遯世无悶。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Chằm ngập cây, là quẻ Đại Quá, đấng quân tử coi đó mà đứng một mình không sợ, trốn đời không buồn.
image.en: (The trigram representing) trees hidden beneath that for the waters of a marsh forms Da Guo. The superior man, in accordance with this, stands up alone and has no fear, and keeps retired from the world without regret.

text.zh: 初六：藉用白茅，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Trải dùng cỏ tranh trắng, không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows one placing mats of the white māo grass under things set on the ground. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九二：枯楊生稊，老夫得其女妻，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Cây Dương khô mọc rễ, chồng già được vợ con gái, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows a decayed willow producing shoots, or an old husband in possession of his young wife. There will be advantage in every way.

text.zh: 九三：棟橈，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Cột ỏe, hung.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows a beam that is weak. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九四：棟隆，吉；有它吝。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: cột cao, tốt; có khí khác, đáng tiếc.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows a beam curving upwards. There will be good fortune. If (the subject of it) looks for other (help but that of line one), there will be cause for regret.

text.zh: 九五：枯楊生華，老婦得士夫，无咎无譽。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Cây dương khô mọc hoa, vợ già được chồng con trai, không lỗi không khen.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows a decayed willow producing flowers, or an old wife in possession of her young husband. There will be occasion neither for blame nor for praise.

text.zh: 上六：過涉滅頂，凶，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Quá lội ngập đỉnh đầu, hung, không lỗi.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject with extraordinary (boldness) wading through a stream, till the water hides the crown of his head. There will be evil, but no ground for blame.

judgment.zh: 坎：習坎，有孚，維心亨，行有尚。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Khảm kép, có tin, bui lòng, hanh, đi có chuộng.
judgment.en: Kan, here repeated, shows the possession of sincerity, through which the mind is penetrating. Action (in accordance with this) will be of high value.

image.zh: 水洊至，習坎；君子以常德行，習教事。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Nước lại tới, là quẻ Khảm kép, đấng quân tử coi đó mà giữ cho đức hạnh có thường, tập cho giáo sự được quen.
image.en: (The representation of) water flowing on continuously forms the repeated Kan. The superior man, in accordance with this, maintains constantly the virtue (of his heart) and (the integrity of) his conduct, and practises the business of instruction.

text.zh: 初六：習坎，入于坎窞，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Chỗ hiểm kép, vào cái hố trong chỗ hiểm, hung.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject in the double defile, and (yet) entering a cavern within it. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九二：坎有險，求小得。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Chỗ trũng có sự hiểm, tìm hơi được.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject in all the peril of the defile. He will, however, get a little (of the deliverance) that he seeks.

text.zh: 六三：來之坎坎，險且枕，入于坎窞，勿用。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Lại đi hiểm hiểm, hiểm vào gối, vào cái hố của hiểm, chớ dùng.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject, whether he comes or goes, confronted by a defile. All is peril to him and unrest. (His endeavours) will lead him into the cavern of the pit. There should be no action (in such a case).

text.zh: 六四：樽酒簋貳，用缶，納約自牖，終无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Chén rượu, rá xôi, thêm dùng hồ sành, nộp ước tự cửa sổ tròn, sau chót không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject (at a feast), with (simply) a bottle of spirits, and a subsidiary basket of rice, while (the cups and bowls) are (only) of earthenware. He introduces his important lessons (as his ruler's) intelligence admits. There will in the end be no error.

text.zh: 九五：坎不盈，祗既平，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Chỗ trũng chẳng đầy, đến đã phẳng, không lỗi.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the water of the defile not yet full, (so that it might flow away); but order will (soon) be brought about. There will be no error.

text.zh: 上六：係用徽纆，寘于叢棘，三歲不得，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Trói dùng chạc thừng, đặt ở bụi gai, ba năm chẳng được, hung.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject bound with cords of three strands or two strands, and placed in the thicket of thorns. But in three years he does not learn the course for him to pursue. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 離：利貞，亨。畜牝牛，吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Ly lợi về sự chính, hanh, nuôi trâu cái, tốt.
judgment.en: Li indicates that, (in regard to what it denotes), it will be advantageous to be firm and correct, and that thus there will be free course and success. Let (its subject) also nourish (a docility like that of) the cow, and there will be good fortune.

image.zh: 明兩作，離；大人以繼明照于四方。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng; Sự sáng hai lần dấy, là quẻ Ly, bậc người lớn coi đó mà kế tiếp sáng soi ở bốn phương.
image.en: (The trigram for) brightness, repeated, forms Li. The great man, in accordance with this, cultivates more and more his brilliant (virtue), and diffuses its brightness over the four quarters (of the land).

text.zh: 初九：履錯然，敬之无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Xéo bừa vậy, kính đó không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows one ready to move with confused steps. But he treads at the same time reverently, and there will be no mistake.

text.zh: 六二：黃離，元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Vàng sáng, cả tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject in his place in yellow. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：日昃之離，不鼓缶而歌，則大耋之嗟，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Sự sáng của mặt trời dé. Chẳng gõ chậu sành mà hát thì là cả già mà than, hung.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject in a position like that of the declining sun. Instead of playing on his instrument of earthenware, and singing to it, he utters the groans of an old man of eighty. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九四：突如其來如，焚如，死如，棄如。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Đột như, thửa lai vậy, cháy vậy, chết vậy, bỏ vậy!
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows the manner of its subject's coming. How abrupt it is, as with fire, with death, to be rejected (by all)!

text.zh: 六五：出涕沱若，戚嗟若，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Nước mắt giàn giụa vậy, ngậm ngùi than vậy, tốt!
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject as one with tears flowing in torrents, and groaning in sorrow. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：王用出征，有嘉折首，獲匪其醜，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Vua dùng ra đánh, có sự tốt, bẻ đầu, bắt chẳng phải loài, không lỗi.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows the king employing its subject in his punitive expeditions. Achieving admirable (merit), he breaks (only) the chiefs (of the rebels). Where his prisoners were not their associates, he does not punish. There will be no error.

judgment.zh: 咸，亨，利貞，取女吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Hàm hanh, lợi chính, lấy con gái, tốt.
judgment.en: Xian indicates that, (on the fulfilment of the conditions implied in it), there will be free course and success. Its advantageousness will depend on the being firm and correct, (as) in marrying a young lady. There will be good fortune.

image.zh: 山上有澤，咸；君子以虛受人。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên núi có chằm, là quẻ Hàm, đấng quân tử coi đó mà trống rỗng lòng mình để nhận người ta.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and above it that for (the waters of) a marsh form Xian. The superior man, in accordance with this, keeps his mind free from pre-occupation, and open to receive (the influences of) others.

text.zh: 初六，咸其拇。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Cảm thửa ngón chân cái.
text.en: The first six, divided, shows one moving his great toes.

text.zh: 六二，咸其腓，凶，居吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Cảm thửa bụng chân, hung. Ở yên, tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows one moving the calves of his leg. There will be evil. If he abide (quiet in his place), there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三，咸其股，執其隨，往吝。
text.vi: Hào Chính Ba: Cảm thửa đùi, giữ thửa sự theo, đi thì đáng tiếc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows one moving his thighs, and keeping close hold of those whom he follows. Going forward (in this way) will cause regret.

text.zh: 九四，貞吉，悔亡，憧憧往來，朋從爾思。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Chính bền thì tốt, sự ăn năn sẽ mất; săng sắc đi lại, bạn theo sự nghĩ của mày.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows that firm correctness which will lead to good fortune, and prevent all occasion for repentance. If its subject be unsettled in his movements, (only) his friends will follow his purpose.

text.zh: 九五，咸其脢，无悔。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Cảm thửa thăn thịt, không ăn năn.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows one moving the flesh along the spine above the heart. There will be no occasion for repentance.

text.zh: 上六，咸其輔頰舌。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Cảm thửa xương má, mép, lưỡi.
text.en: The sixth six, divided, shows one moving his jaws and tongue.

judgment.zh: 恆：亨，无咎，利貞，利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Hằng hanh, lợi về sự chính, lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Heng indicates successful progress and no error (in what it denotes). But the advantage will come from being firm and correct; and movement in any direction whatever will be advantageous.

image.zh: 雷風，恆；君子以立不易方。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sấm gió là quẻ Hằng, đấng quân tử coi đó mà đứng chẳng đổi phương.
image.en: (The trigram representing) thunder and that for wind form Heng. The superior man, in accordance with this, stands firm, and does not change his method (of operation).

text.zh: 初六：浚恆，貞凶，无攸利。
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject deeply (desirous) of long continuance. Even with firm correctness there will be evil; there will be no advantage in any way.

text.zh: 九二：悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Sự ăn năn mất.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows all occasion for repentance disappearing.

text.zh: 九三：不恆其德，或承之羞，貞吝。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Chẳng thường thửa đức, hoặc vâng đấy thẹn, trinh cùng đáng tiếc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows one who does not continuously maintain his virtue. There are those who will impute this to him as a disgrace. However firm he may be, there will be ground for regret.

text.zh: 九四：田无禽。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Săn không loài cầm.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows a field where there is no game.

text.zh: 六五：恆其德，貞，婦人吉，夫子凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Thường thửa đức, chính, đàn bà tốt, đàn ông hung.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject continuously maintaining the virtue indicated by it. In a wife this will be fortunate; in a husband, evil.

text.zh: 上六：振恆，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Xốc thường, hung.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject exciting himself to long continuance. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 遯：亨，小利貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ độn hanh, nhỏ lợi trinh.
judgment.en: Dun indicates successful progress (in its circumstances). To a small extent it will (still) be advantageous to be firm and correct.

image.zh: 天下有山，遯；君子以遠小人，不惡而嚴。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới trời có núi, là quẻ Độn, đấng quân tử coi đó mà xa kẻ tiểu nhân, không ác mà nghiêm.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the sky and below it that for a mountain form Dun. The superior man, in accordance with this, keeps small men at a distance, not by showing that he hates them, but by his own dignified gravity.

text.zh: 初六：遯尾，厲，勿用有攸往。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Trốn đuôi, nguy. Chớ dùng, có thửa đi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows a retiring tail. The position is perilous. No movement in any direction should be made.

text.zh: 六二：執之用黃牛之革，莫之勝說。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Giữ đó, dùng da trâu vàng, không ai trút nổi?
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject holding (his purpose) fast as if by a (thong made from the) hide of a yellow ox, which cannot be broken.

text.zh: 九三：係遯，有疾厲，畜臣妾吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Vướng trốn, có tật, nguy. Nuôi đầy tớ nàng hầu, tốt.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows one retiring but bound,--to his distress and peril. (If he were to deal with his binders as in) nourishing a servant or concubine, it would be fortunate for him.

text.zh: 九四：好遯，君子吉，小人否。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Yêu việc trốn, đấng quân tử tốt, kẻ tiểu nhân không.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring notwithstanding his likings. In a superior man this will lead to good fortune; a small man cannot attain to this.

text.zh: 九五：嘉遯，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Trốn tốt, chính tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in an admirable way. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：肥遯，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Trốn hay, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject retiring in a noble way. It will be advantageous in every respect.

judgment.zh: 大壯：利貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Đại Tráng lợi về sự chính.
judgment.en: Da Zhuang indicates that (under the conditions which it symbolises) it will be advantageous to be firm and correct.

image.zh: 雷在天上，大壯；君子以非禮弗履。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sấm ở trên trời, là quẻ Đại Tráng. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà không phải lễ chớ xéo vào.
image.en: (The trigram representing) heaven and above it that for thunder form Da Zhuang. The superior man, in accordance with this, does not take a step which is not according to propriety.

text.zh: 初九：壯于趾，征凶，有孚。
