text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Mạnh ở ngón chân, đi thì hung, có tin.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject manifesting his strength in his toes. But advance will lead to evil,--most certainly.

text.zh: 九二：貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Chính tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows that with firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：小人用壯，君子用罔，貞厲。羝羊觸藩，羸其角。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Kẻ tiểu nhân dùng sức mạnh, đấng quân tử dùng chẳng [kiêng dè], chính, nguy. Dê đực húc giậu, mắc thửa sừng.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows, in the case of a small man, one using all his strength; and in the case of a superior man, one whose rule is not to do so. Even with firm correctness the position would be perilous.

text.zh: 九四：貞吉悔亡，藩決不羸，壯于大輿之輹。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Chính thì tốt, ăn năn mất, phên bựt chẳng mắc, mạnh ở vành trục xe lớn.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows (a case in which) firm correctness leads to good fortune, and occasion for repentance disappears. (We see) the fence opened without the horns being entangled.

text.zh: 六五：喪羊于易，无悔。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Mất dê ở sự dễ, không ăn năn.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows one who loses his ram(-like strength) in the ease of his position. (But) there will be no occasion for repentance.

text.zh: 上六：羝羊觸藩，不能退，不能遂，无攸利，艱則吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Dê đực húc giậu, chẳng hay lui, chẳng hay toại, không thửa lợi, khó thì tốt.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows (one who may be compared to) the ram butting against the fence, and unable either to retreat, or to advance as he would fain do. There will not be advantage in any respect; but if he realise the difficulty (of his position), there will be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 晉：康侯用錫馬蕃庶，晝日三接。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tấn, tước hầu yên dừng cho ngựa giậm nhiều, ban ngày ba lần tiếp.
judgment.en: In Jin we see a prince who secures the tranquillity (of the people) presented on that account with numerous horses (by the king), and three times in a day received at interviews.

image.zh: 明出地上，晉；君子以自昭明德。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Lửa ở trên đất, là quẻ Tấn, đấng quân tử coi đó để tự làm tỏ đức sáng.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth and that for the bright (sun) coming forth above it form Jin. The superior man, according to this, gives himself to make more brilliant his bright virtue.

text.zh: 初六：晉如，摧如，貞吉。罔孚，裕无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu; Dường tiến lên vậy, dường nén lui vậy, chính thì tốt, chẳng tin, khoan thai không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows one wishing to advance, and (at the same time) kept back. Let him be firm and correct, and there will be good fortune. If trust be not reposed in him, let him maintain a large and generous mind, and there will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：晉如，愁如，貞吉。受茲介福，于其王母。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Dường tiến vậy, dường sầu vậy, chính thì tốt, nhận phúc lớn ấy chưng thuở bà nội.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject with the appearance of advancing, and yet of being sorrowful. If he be firm and correct, there will be good fortune. He will receive this great blessing from his grandmother.

text.zh: 六三：眾允，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Mọi người tin, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject trusted by all (around him). All occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 九四：晉如碩鼠，貞厲。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Dường tiến vậy, con chuột đồng, chính bền, nguy!
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject with the appearance of advancing, but like a marmot. However firm and correct he may be, the position is one of peril.

text.zh: 六五：悔亡，失得勿恤，往吉，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Ăn năn mất, mất được chớ lo, đi thì tốt, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows how all occasion for repentance disappears (from its subject). (But) let him not concern himself about whether he shall fail or succeed. To advance will be fortunate, and in every way advantageous.

text.zh: 上九：晉其角，維用伐邑，厲吉无咎，貞吝。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Tiến thửa sừng, bui dùng đánh làng, tốt, không lỗi, trinh thì đáng tiếc.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows one advancing his horns. But he only uses them to punish the (rebellious people of his own) city. The position is perilous, but there will be good fortune. (Yet) however firm and correct he may be, there will be occasion for regret.

judgment.zh: 明夷：利艱貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Minh Di lợi về khó nhọc trinh chính.
judgment.en: Ming Yi indicates that (in the circumstances which it denotes) it will be advantageous to realise the difficulty (of the position), and maintain firm correctness.

image.zh: 明入地中，明夷；君子以蒞眾，用晦而明。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sáng vào trong đất là quẻ Minh Di. Đấng quân tử coi đó để trị dân chúng, dùng tối mà sáng.
image.en: (The trigram representing) the earth and that for the bright (sun) entering within it form Ming Yi. The superior man, in accordance with this, conducts his management of men; - he shows his intelligence by keeping it obscured.

text.zh: 初九：明夷于飛，垂其翼。君子于行，三日不食，有攸往，主人有言。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Sáng đau chưng bay, đủ thửa cánh; đấng quân tử chưng đi, ba ngày chẳng ăn, có thửa đi, người chủ có nói.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject, (in the condition indicated by) Ming Yi, flying, but with drooping wings. When the superior man (is revolving) his going away, he may be for three days without eating.

text.zh: 六二：明夷，夷于左股，用拯馬壯，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Sáng đau, đau ở đùi bên tả, dùng cách cứu, ngựa mạnh tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject, (in the condition indicated by) Ming Yi, wounded in the left thigh. He saves himself by the strength of a (swift) horse; and is fortunate.

text.zh: 九三：明夷于南狩，得其大首，不可疾貞。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Sáng đau chưng cuộc săn bên Nam được thừa đầu lớn, chẳng khá kíp chính.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject, (in the condition indicated by) Ming Yi, hunting in the south, and taking the great chief (of the darkness). He should not be eager to make (all) correct (at once).

text.zh: 六四：入于左腹，獲明夷之心，出于門庭。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Vào chưng bụng bên tả được cái lòng sáng đau, chưng ra cửa sân.
text.en: The fourth six, divided, shows its subject (just) entered into the left side of the belly (of the dark land). (But) he is able to carry out the mind appropriate (in the condition indicated by) Ming Yi, quitting the gate and courtyard (of the lord of darkness).

text.zh: 六五：箕子之明夷，利貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm, ông Cơ Tử chưng sáng đau, lợi về chính bền.
text.en: The fifth six, divided, shows how the Count of K'i fulfilled the condition indicated by Ming Yi. It will be advantageous to be firm and correct.

text.zh: 上六：不明晦，初登于天，後入于地。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Chẳng sáng, tối; đầu thì lên chưng trời, sau vào chưng đất.
text.en: The sixth six, divided, shows the case where there is no light, but (only) obscurity. (Its subject) had at first ascended to (the top of) the sky; his future shall be to go into the earth.

judgment.zh: 家人：利女貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Gia Nhân lợi về gái chính.
judgment.en: For (the realisation of what is taught in) Jia Ren, (or for the regulation of the family), what is most advantageous is that the wife be firm and correct.

image.zh: 風自火出，家人；君子以言有物，而行有恆。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gió từ lửa ra, là quẻ Gia Nhân, đấng quân tử coi đó mà nói có vật, nết có thường.
image.en: (The trigram representing) fire, and that for wind coming forth from it, form Jia Ren. The superior man, in accordance with this, orders his words according to (the truth of) things, and his conduct so that it is uniformly consistent.

text.zh: 初九：閑有家，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Ngăn ngừa có nhà, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject establishing restrictive regulations in his household. Occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 六二：无攸遂，在中饋，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: không thửa thỏa, ở trong, chủ việc ăn uống, chính tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject taking nothing on herself, but in her central place attending to the preparation of the food. Through her firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：家人嗃嗃，悔厲吉；婦子嘻嘻，終吝。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Người nhà nem nép (?), hối dữ, tốt; vợ con hơn hớn, sau chót thẹn tiếc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject (treating) the members of the household with stern severity. There will be occasion for repentance, there will be peril, (but) there will (also) be good fortune.

text.zh: 六四：富家，大吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Nhà giàu, cả tốt.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject enriching the family. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：王假有家，勿恤，往吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Vua đến có nhà, chớ lo, tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the influence of the king extending to his family. There need be no anxiety; there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：有孚威如，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Có tin, dường oai nghiêm vậy, trọn tốt,
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject possessed of sincerity and arrayed in majesty. In the end there will be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 睽：小事吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Khuê, việc nhỏ tốt.
judgment.en: Kui indicates that, (notwithstanding the condition of things which it denotes), in small matters there will (still) be good success.

image.zh: 上火下澤，睽；君子以同而異。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên lửa, dưới chằm, là quẻ Khuê. Đấng quân tử coi đó để cùng mà khác.
image.en: (The trigram representing) fire above, and that for (the waters of) a marsh below, form Kui. The superior man, in accordance with this, where there is a general agreement, yet admits diversity.

text.zh: 初九：悔亡，喪馬勿逐，自復；見惡人无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Ăn năn mất, mất ngựa, chớ đuổi theo tự nhiên trở lại, thấy người ác, không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows that (to its subject) occasion for repentance will disappear. He has lost his horses, but let him not seek for them - they will return of themselves. Should he meet with bad men, he will not err (in communicating with them).

text.zh: 九二：遇主于巷，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Gặp chúa ở ngõ, không lỗi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject happening to meet with his lord in a bye-passage. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六三：見輿曳，其牛掣，其人天且劓，无初有終。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Thấy xe kéo, thửa trâu kìm, thửa người gọt đầu và xẻo mũi. Không lúc đầu, có chót.
text.en: In the third SIX, divided, we see one whose carriage is dragged back, while the oxen in it are pushed back, and he is himself subjected to the shaving of his head and the cutting off of his nose. There is no good beginning, but there will be a good end.

text.zh: 九四：睽孤，遇元夫，交孚，厲无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Lìa côi, gặp chàng lành, tin lẫn, nguy, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject solitary amidst the (prevailing) disunion. (But) he meets with the good man (represented by the first line), and they blend their sincere desires together. The position is one of peril, but there will be no mistake.

text.zh: 六五：悔亡，厥宗噬膚，往何咎。
text.vi: Ăn năn mất, thửa họ cắn da, đi có lỗi gì?
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows that (to its subject) occasion for repentance will disappear. With his relative (and minister he unites closely and readily) as if he were biting through a piece of skin. When he goes forward (with this help), what error can there be?

text.zh: 上九：睽孤，見豕負塗，載鬼一車，先張之弧，後說之弧，匪寇婚媾，往遇雨則吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Lìa cô. Thấy lợn đội bùn, chở ma một xe, trước giương chưng cung, sau tháo chưng cung, chẳng phải giặc, là dâu gia đó, đi gặp mưa thì tốt.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject solitary amidst the (prevailing) disunion. (In the subject of the third line, he seems to) see a pig bearing on its back a load of mud, (or fancies) there is a carriage full of ghosts. He first bends his bow against him, and afterwards unbends it, (for he discovers) that he is not an assailant to injure, but a near relative. Going forward, he shall meet with (genial) rain, and there will be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 蹇：利西南，不利東北；利見大人，貞吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Kiển, lợi Tây Nam không lợi Đông Bắc, lợi về sự thấy người lớn, chính thì tốt.
judgment.en: In (the state indicated by) Jian advantage will be found in the south-west, and the contrary in the north-east. It will be advantageous (also) to meet with the great man.

image.zh: 山上有水，蹇；君子以反身脩德。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên núi có nước, là quẻ Kiển, đấng quân tử coi đó mà trở lại mình, sửa đức.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain, and above it that for water, form Jian. The superior man, in accordance with this, turns round (and examines) himself, and cultivates his virtue.

text.zh: 初六：往蹇，來譽。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Đi thì kiển, trở lại được tiếng khen.
text.en: From the first SIX, divided, we learn that advance (on the part of its subject) will lead to (greater) difficulties, while remaining stationary will afford ground for praise.

text.zh: 六二：王臣蹇蹇，匪躬之故。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Bề tôi kiển kiển, chẳng phải cớ của mình.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows the minister of the king struggling with difficulty on difficulty, and not with a view to his own advantage.

text.zh: 九三：往蹇來反。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đi thì kiển, nên trở lại.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject advancing, (but only) to (greater) difficulties. He remains stationary, and returns (to his former associates).

text.zh: 六四：往蹇來連。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đi thì kiển, trở lại thì liền.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject advancing, (but only) to (greater) difficulties. He remains stationary, and unites (with the subject of the line above).

text.zh: 九五：大蹇朋來。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Kiển lớn, bạn lại.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject struggling with the greatest difficulties, while friends are coming to help him.

text.zh: 上六：往蹇來碩，吉；利見大人。
text.vi: Hào Sáu trên: Đi thì kiển, trở lại được lợi lớn, tốt; lợi thấy người lớn.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject going forward, (only to increase) the difficulties, while his remaining stationary will be (productive of) great (merit).

judgment.zh: 解：利西南，无所往，其來復吉。有攸往，夙吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Giải lợi về phương Tây Nam, không thửa đi thì lại lại, có thửa đi, sớm thì tốt.
judgment.en: In (the state indicated by) Jie advantage will be found in the south-west. If no (further) operations be called for, there will be good fortune in coming back (to the old conditions). If some operations be called for, there will be good fortune in the early conducting of them.

image.zh: 雷雨作，解；君子以赦過宥罪。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Sấm mưa nổi lên, là quẻ Giải. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà xá lỗi, tha tội.
image.en: (The trigram representing) thunder and that for rain, with these phenomena in a state of manifestation, form Jie. The superior man, in accordance with this, forgives errors, and deals gently with crimes.

text.zh: 初六：无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows that its subject will commit no error.

text.zh: 九二：田獲三狐，得黃矢，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Săn được ba con cáo, được tên vàng, chính bền thì tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject catch, in hunting, three foxes, and obtain the yellow (= golden) arrows. With firm correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：負且乘，致寇至，貞吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Đội và cưỡi, dắt giặc đến, chính bền cũng đáng tiếc.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows a porter with his burden, (yet) riding in a carriage. He will (only) tempt robbers to attack him. However firm and correct he may (try to) be, there will be cause for regret.

text.zh: 九四：解而拇，朋至斯孚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Giải ngón chân cái mày, bằng đến ấy tin.
text.en: (To the subject of) the fourth NINE, undivided, (it is said), 'Remove your toes. Friends will (then) come, between you and whom there will be mutual confidence.'

text.zh: 六五：君子維有解，吉；有孚于小人。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Đấng quân tử chỉ có giải, tốt, có tin chưng kẻ tiểu nhân.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows (its subject), the superior man (= the ruler), executing his function of removing (whatever is injurious to the idea of the hexagram), in which case there will he good fortune, and confidence in him will be shown even by the small men.

text.zh: 上六：公用射隼于高墉之上，獲之，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Ông dùng bắn chim cắt ở trên tường cao, được chim, không gì không lợi.
text.en: In the sixth SIX, divided, we see a feudal prince (with his bow) shooting at a falcon on the top of a high wall, and hitting it. (The effect of his action) will be in every way advantageous.

judgment.zh: 損：有孚，元吉，无咎，可貞，利有攸往。曷之用，二簋可用享。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tổn, có tin, cả tốt, không lỗi, khá trinh, lợi có thửa đi. Chưng dùng gì? Hai quĩ khá dùng hưởng.
judgment.en: In (what is denoted by) Sun, if there be sincerity (in him who employs it), there will be great good fortune:--freedom from error; firmness and correctness that can be maintained; and advantage in every movement that shall be made. In what shall this (sincerity in the exercise of Sun) be employed? (Even) in sacrifice two baskets of grain, (though there be nothing else), may be presented.

image.zh: 山下有澤，損；君子以懲忿窒欲。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới núi có chằm, là quẻ Tổn đấng quân tử coi đó mà răn sự giận, lấp lòng dục.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and beneath it that for the waters of a marsh form Sun. The superior man, in accordance with this, restrains his wrath and represses his desires.

text.zh: 初九：巳事遄往，无咎，酌損之。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Xong việc, mau đi, không lỗi, châm chước mà bớt đấy.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject suspending his own affairs, and hurrying away (to help the subject of the fourth line). He will commit no error, but let him consider how far he should contribute of what is his (for the other).

text.zh: 九二：利貞，征凶，弗損，益之。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Lợi về chính bền, đi thì hung, chớ bớt, thêm đấy.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows that it will be advantageous for its subject to maintain a firm correctness, and that action on his part will be evil. He can give increase (to his correlate) without taking from himself.

text.zh: 六三：三人行，則損一人；一人行，則得其友。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Ba người đi thì tổn một người, một người đi thì được thửa bạn.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows how of three men walking together, the number is diminished by one; and how one, walking, finds his friend.

text.zh: 六四：損其疾，使遄有喜，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Bớt thửa tật, khiến chóng có mừng, cả tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject diminishing the ailment under which he labours by making (the subject of the first line) hasten (to his help), and make him glad. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六五：或益之，十朋之龜弗克違，元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Hoặc ích cho đấy, chưng con rùa mười “bằng” chẳng hay trái, cả tốt!
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows parties adding to (the stores of) its subject ten pairs of tortoise shells, and accepting no refusal. There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：弗損益之，无咎，貞吉，有攸往，得臣无家。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Chẳng bớt, thêm đấy, không lỗi, chính bền tốt, lợi có thửa đi, được bề tôi không nhà.
text.en: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject giving increase to others without taking from himself. There will be no error. With firm correctness there will be good fortune. There will be advantage in every movement that shall be made. He will find ministers more than can be counted by their clans.

judgment.zh: 益：利有攸往，利涉大川。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Ích lợi có thửa đi, lợi về sang sông lớn.
judgment.en: Yi indicates that (in the state which it denotes) there will be advantage in every movement which shall be undertaken, that it will be advantageous (even) to cross the great stream.

image.zh: 風雷，益；君子以見善則遷，有過則改。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gió sấm là quẻ Ích, đấng quân tử coi đó mà thấy điều thiện thì dời sang, có lỗi thì đổi đi.
image.en: (The trigram representing) wind and that for thunder form Yi. The superior man, in accordance with this, when he sees what is good, moves towards it; and when he sees his errors, he turns from them.

text.zh: 初九：利用為大作，元吉，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Lợi dùng làm việc lớn, cả tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows that it will be advantageous for its position to make a great movement. If it be greatly fortunate, no blame will be imputed to him.

text.zh: 六二：或益之，十朋之龜弗克違，永貞吉。王用享于帝，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Hoặc ích đấy, chưng rùa mười bằng, chẳng hay trái, vĩnh viễn chính bền, tốt, vua dùng hưởng chưng trời, tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows parties adding to the stores of its subject ten pairs of tortoise shells whose oracles cannot be opposed. Let him persevere in being firm and correct, and there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：益之用凶事，无咎。有孚中行，告公用圭。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Ích đây, dùng việc hung, không lỗi, có tin, đường giữa, bảo tước công dùng ngọc khuê.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows increase given to its subject by means of what is evil, so that he shall (be led to good), and be without blame.

text.zh: 六四：中行，告公從。利用為依遷國。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đường giữa, tâu tước công theo, lợi dụng làm tựa, dời nước.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject pursuing the due course. His advice to his prince is followed. He can with advantage be relied on in such a movement as that of removing the capital.

text.zh: 九五：有孚惠心，勿問元吉。有孚惠我德。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Có tin lòng ơn, chớ hỏi, cả tốt, có tin, ơn đức ta.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject with sincere heart seeking to benefit (all below). There need be no question about it; the result will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：莫益之，或擊之，立心勿恆，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Chẳng ích nó, hoặc đánh nó, lập tâm chớ thường hung.
text.en: In the sixth NINE, undivided, we see one to whose increase none will contribute, while many will seek to assail him. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 夬：揚于王庭，孚號，有厲，告自邑，不利即戎，利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Quải, giơ chưng sân vua, tin gọi, có nguy. Báo từ làng, chẳng lợi tới quân, lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: Guai requires (in him who would fulfil its meaning) the exhibition (of the culprit's guilt) in the royal court, and a sincere and earnest appeal (for sympathy and support), with a consciousness of the peril (involved in cutting off the criminal). He should (also) make announcement in his own city, and show that it will not be well to have recourse at once to arms. (In this way) there will be advantage in whatever he shall go forward to.

image.zh: 澤上於天，夬；君子以施祿及下，居德則忌。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Chằm lên chưng trời, là quẻ Quải. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà ra lộc tới dưới, ở đức phép kỵ.
image.en: (The trigram representing) heaven and that for the waters of a marsh mounting above it form Guai. The superior man, in accordance with this, bestows emolument on those below him, and dislikes allowing his gifts to accumulate (undispensed).

text.zh: 初九：壯于前趾，往不勝為咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Mạnh chưng ngón chân trước, đi thẳng được, là lỗi.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject in (the pride of) strength advancing with his toes. He goes forward, but will not succeed. There will be ground for blame.

text.zh: 九二：惕號，莫夜有戎，勿恤。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Sợ kêu, đêm hôm có quân, chớ ngại.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject full of apprehension and appealing (for sympathy and help). Late at night hostile measures may be (taken against him), but he need not be anxious about them.

text.zh: 九三：壯于頄，有凶。君子夬夬，獨行，遇雨，若濡，有慍，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Mạnh chưng gò má, có sự hung, đấng quân tử quyết quyết, đi một mình gặp mưa, đường ướt, có giận, không lỗi.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject (about to advance) with strong (and determined) looks. There will be evil. (But) the superior man, bent on cutting off (the criminal), will walk alone and encounter the rain, (till he be hated by his proper associates) as if he were contaminated (by the others). (In the end) there will be no blame against him.

text.zh: 九四：臀无膚，其行次且。牽羊悔亡，聞言不信。
text.vi: Hào Chin Tư: Đít không da, thửa đi chật vật, dắt dê, ăn năn mất, nghe nói chẳng tin.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows one from whose buttocks the skin has been stripped, and who walks slowly and with difficulty. (If he could act) like a sheep led (after its companions), occasion for repentance would disappear. But though he hear these words, he will not believe them.

text.zh: 九五：莧陸夬夬，中行无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Rau sam quyết quyết, đi đường giữa không lỗi.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows (the small men like) a bed of purslain, which ought to be uprooted with the utmost determination. (The subject of the line having such determination), his action, in harmony with his central position, will lead to no error or blame.

text.zh: 上六：无號，終有凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Không kêu, sau chót có hung.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows its subject without any (helpers) on whom to call. His end will be evil.

judgment.zh: 姤：女壯，勿用取女。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Cấu, con gái mạnh, chớ dùng lấy con gái.
judgment.en: Gou shows a female who is bold and strong. It will not be good to marry (such) a female.

image.zh: 天下有風，姤；后以施命誥四方。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Dưới trời có gió, là quẻ Cấu vui coi đó mà ra mệnh bảo bốn phương.
image.en: (The trigram representing) wind and that for the sky above it form Gou. The sovereign, in accordance with this, delivers his charges, and promulgates his announcements throughout the four quarters (of the kingdom).

text.zh: 初六：繫于金柅，貞吉，有攸往，見凶，羸豕孚蹢躅。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Buộc chưng neo sắt, chính tốt, có thửa đi, thấy hung, con lợn còm tin nhảy nhót.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows how its subject should be kept (like a carriage) tied and fastened to a metal drag, in which case with firm correctness there will be good fortune. (But) if he move in any direction, evil will appear. He will be (like) a lean pig, which is sure to keep jumping about.

text.zh: 九二：包有魚，无咎，不利賓。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Bọc có cá, không lỗi chẳng lợi khách.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject with a wallet of fish. There will be no error. But it will not be well to let (the subject of the first line) go forward to the guests.

text.zh: 九三：臀无膚，其行次且，厲，无大咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đít không da, thửa đi chật vật, nguy, không lỗi lớn.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows one from whose buttocks the skin has been stripped so that he walks with difficulty. The position is perilous, but there will be no great error.

text.zh: 九四：包无魚，起凶。
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject with his wallet, but no fish in it. This will give rise to evil.

text.zh: 九五：以杞包瓜，含章，有隕自天。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: lấy cây kỷ bọc quả dưa, ngậm văn vẻ, có sa tự trời.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, (shows its subject as) a medlar tree overspreading the gourd (beneath it). If he keep his brilliant qualities concealed, (a good issue) will descend (as) from Heaven.

text.zh: 上九：姤其角，吝，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Gặp thửa sừng, đáng tiếc, không lỗi.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject receiving others on his horns. There will be occasion for regret, but there will be no error.

judgment.zh: 萃：亨。王假有廟，利見大人，亨，利貞。用大牲吉，利有攸往。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tụy hanh, vua đến có miếu. Lợi thấy bậc người lớn, hạnh, lợi về chính bền. Dùng con sinh, lớn, tốt, lợi có thửa đi.
judgment.en: In (the state denoted by) Cui, the king will repair to his ancestral temple. It will be advantageous (also) to meet with the great man; and then there will be progress and success, though the advantage must come through firm correctness. The use of great victims will conduce to good fortune; and in whatever direction movement is made, it will be advantageous.

image.zh: 澤上於地，萃；君子以除戎器，戒不虞。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Chằm lên chưng đất, là quẻ Tụy, đấng quân tử coi đó mà trừ đồ quân, răn sự chẳng lường.
image.en: (The trigram representing the) earth and that for the waters of a marsh raised above it form Cui. The superior man, in accordance with this, has his weapons of war put in good repair, to be prepared against unforeseen contingencies.

text.zh: 初六：有孚不終，乃亂乃萃，若號一握為笑，勿恤，往无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Có tin, không chót, bèn loạn, bèn họp, bằng kêu, một nắm làm cười, chớ lo, đi không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject with a sincere desire (for union), but unable to carry it out, so that disorder is brought into the sphere of his union. If he cry out (for help to his proper correlate), all at once (his tears) will give place to smiles. He need not mind (the temporary difficulty); as he goes forward, there will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：引吉，无咎，孚乃利用禴。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Dẫn tốt, không lỗi, tin bèn lợi, dùng tế thược.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject led forward (by his correlate). There will be good fortune, and freedom from error. There is entire sincerity, and in that case (even the small offerings of) the vernal sacrifice are acceptable.

text.zh: 六三：萃如，嗟如，无攸利，往无咎，小吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Dường họp vậy, dường than vậy không thửa lợi, đi không lỗi, hơi tiếc.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject striving after union and seeming to sigh, yet nowhere finding any advantage. If he go forward, he will not err, though there may be some small cause for regret.

text.zh: 九四：大吉，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Cả tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject in such a state that, if he be greatly fortunate, he will receive no blame.

text.zh: 九五：萃有位，无咎。匪孚，元永貞，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Họp có ngôi, không lỗi, chẳng tin, cả, dài, chính, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the union (of all) under its subject in the place of dignity. There will be no error. If any do not have confidence in him, let him see to it that (his virtue) be great, long-continued, and firmly correct, and all occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 上六：齎咨涕洟，无咎。
text.vi: Háo Sáu Trên: Than thở, nước mắt, nước mũi, không lỗi.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject sighing and weeping; but there will be no error.

judgment.zh: 升：元亨，用見大人，勿恤，南征吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Thăng, cả hanh, dùng thấy người lớn, chớ lo, đi về phương Nam tốt.
judgment.en: Sheng indicates that (under its conditions) there will be great progress and success. Seeking by (the qualities implied in it) to meet with the great man, its subject need have no anxiety. Advance to the south will be fortunate.

image.zh: 地中生木，升；君子以順德，積小以高大。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trong đất mọc cây là quẻ Thăng, đấng quân tử coi đó mà thuận đức, chứa điều nhỏ để cao lớn.
image.en: (The trigram representing) wood and that for the earth with the wood growing in the midst of it form Sheng. The superior man, in accordance with this, pays careful attention to his virtue, and accumulates the small developments of it till it is high and great.

text.zh: 初六：允升，大吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Tin lên, cả tốt.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject advancing upwards with the welcome (of those above him). There will be great good fortune.

text.zh: 九二：孚乃利用禴，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Tin bèn lợi, dùng tế thược, không lỗi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject with that sincerity which will make even the (small) offerings of the vernal sacrifice acceptable. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九三：升虛邑。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Lên làng trống không.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject ascending upwards (as into) an empty city.

text.zh: 六四：王用亨于岐山，吉无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Vua dùng hưởng ở núi Kỳ, tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject employed by the king to present his offerings on mount Khi. There will be good fortune; there will be no mistake.

text.zh: 六五：貞吉，升階。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Chính bền, tốt, lên thềm.
text.en: The fifth six, divided, shows its subject firmly correct, and therefore enjoying good fortune. He ascends the stairs (with all due ceremony).

text.zh: 上六：冥升，利于不息之貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Tối sự lên, lợi về sự chính bền chẳng nghỉ.
text.en: The sixth six, divided, shows its subject advancing upwards blindly. Advantage will be found in a ceaseless maintenance of firm correctness.

judgment.zh: 困：亨，貞，大人吉，无咎，有言不信。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Khốn hanh, chính bền, người lớn tốt, không lỗi, có nói không tin.
judgment.en: In (the condition denoted by) Kun there may (yet be) progress and success. For the firm and correct, the (really) great man, there will be good fortune. He will fall into no error. If he make speeches, his words cannot be made good.

image.zh: 澤无水，困；君子以致命遂志。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Chằm không nước là quẻ Khốn, đấng quân tử coi đó mà đến mệnh thỏa chí.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a marsh, and (below it that for a defile, which has drained the other dry so that there is) no water in it, form Kun. The superior man, in accordance with this, will sacrifice his life in order to carry out his purpose.

text.zh: 初六：臀困于株木，入于幽谷，三歲不覿。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Đít khốn chưng trồi cây, vào chưng hang tối, ba năm chẳng thấy.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject with bare buttocks straitened under the stump of a tree. He enters a dark valley, and for three years has no prospect (of deliverance).

text.zh: 九二：困于酒食，朱紱方來，利用享祀，征凶，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Khốn chưng rượu cơm, cái phất đỏ đương lại, lợi dùng cúng tế, đi hung không lỗi.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows its subject straitened amidst his wine and viands. There come to him anon the red knee-covers (of the ruler). It will be well for him (to maintain his sincerity as) in sacrificing. Active operations (on his part) will lead to evil, but he will be free from blame.

text.zh: 六三：困于石，據于蒺蔾，入于其宮，不見其妻，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Khốn chưng đá, vin chưng cây cà gai, vào chưng thửa buồng, chẳng thấy thửa vợ, hung.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject straitened before a (frowning) rock. He lays hold of thorns. He enters his palace, and does not see his wife. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九四：來徐徐，困于金車，吝，有終。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Lại thong thả, khốn chưng xe sắt, đáng tiếc, có chót.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided shows its subject proceeding very slowly (to help the subject of the first line), who is straitened by the carriage adorned with metal in front of him.

text.zh: 九五：劓刖，困于赤紱，乃徐有說，利用祭祀。
text.vi: Xẻo mũi, chặt chân, khốn chưng cái phất đỏ, bèn thong thả có đẹp lòng, lợi dùng tế tự.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject with his nose and feet cut off. He is straitened by (his ministers in their) scarlet aprons. He is leisurely in his movements, however, and is satisfied.

text.zh: 上六：困于葛藟，于臲卼，曰動悔。有悔，征吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Khốn chưng cây sắn dây nhưng cheo leo, rằng: động ăn năn, có ăn năn, đi tốt.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows its subject straitened, as if bound with creepers; or in a high and dangerous position, and saying (to himself), 'If I move, I shall repent it.'

judgment.zh: 井：改邑不改井，无喪无得，往來井井。汔至，亦未繘井，羸其瓶，凶。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tỉnh, đổi làng chẳng đổi giếng, không được, không mất, đi lại giếng giếng. Hầu đến, cũng chưa dong trạc đến giếng, hỏng thửa lọ, hung.
judgment.en: (Looking at) Jing, (we think of) how (the site of) a town may be changed, while (the fashion of) its wells undergoes no change. (The water of a well) never disappears and never receives (any great) increase, and those who come and those who go can draw and enjoy the benefit. If (the drawing) have nearly been accomplished, but, before the rope has quite reached the water, the bucket is broken, this is evil.

image.zh: 木上有水，井；君子以勞民勸相。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên cây có nước là quẻ Tỉnh, đấng quân tử coi đó mà yên ủi dân, khuyến khích sự giúp đỡ.
image.en: (The trigram representing) wood and above it that for water form Jing. The superior man, in accordance with this, comforts the people, and stimulates them to mutual helpfulness.

text.zh: 初六：井泥不食，舊井无禽。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Giếng bùn chẳng ăn, giếng cũ không chim.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows a well so muddy that men will not drink of it; or an old well to which neither birds (nor other creatures) resort.

text.zh: 九二：井谷射鮒，瓮敝漏。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Giếng hang, bắn loài ếch nhái, vò nát dò.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows a well from which by a hole the water escapes and flows away to the shrimps (and such small creatures among the grass), or one the water of which leaks away from a broken basket.

text.zh: 九三：井渫不食，為我心惻，可用汲，王明，並受其福。
text.vi: Hào chín Ba: Giếng trong chẳng ăn, làm sự bùi ngùi cho lòng ta, khá dùng múc nước; vua sáng, cùng chịu thửa phúc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows a well, which has been cleared out, but is not used. Our hearts are sorry for this, for the water might be drawn out and used. If the king were (only) intelligent, both he and we might receive the benefit of it.

text.zh: 六四：井甃，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Giếng xây bờ, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows a well, the lining of which is well laid. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九五：井冽，寒泉食。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Giếng mát, suối lạnh, ăn.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows a clear, limpid well, (the waters from) whose cold spring are (freely) drunk.

text.zh: 上六：井收勿幕，有孚元吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Giếng thu, chớ trùm, có tin, cả tốt.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows (the water from) the well brought to the top, which is not allowed to be covered. This suggests the idea of sincerity. There will be great good fortune.

judgment.zh: 革：巳日乃孚，元亨利貞，悔亡。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Cách, hết ngày bèn tin, cả hanh lợi trinh, ăn năn mất.
judgment.en: (What takes place as indicated by) Ge is believed in only after it has been accomplished. There will be great progress and success. Advantage will come from being firm and correct. (In that case) occasion for repentance will disappear.

image.zh: 澤中有火，革；君子以治曆明時。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trong chằm có lửa, là quẻ Cách. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà trị lịch rõ mùa.
image.en: (The trigram representing the waters of) a marsh and that for fire in the midst of them form Ge. The superior man, in accordance with this, regulates his (astronomical) calculations, and makes clear the seasons and times.

text.zh: 初九：鞏用黃牛之革。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Giàng bó dùng da trâu vàng.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as if he were) bound with the skin of a yellow ox.

text.zh: 六二：巳日乃革之，征吉，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Hết ngày bèn đổi đấy, đi tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject making his changes after some time has passed. Action taken will be fortunate. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九三：征凶，貞厲，革言三就，有孚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đi hung, chính nguy, nói đổi ba lần nên có tin.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows that action taken by its subject will be evil. Though he be firm and correct, his position is perilous. If the change (he contemplates) have been three times fully discussed, he will be believed in.

text.zh: 九四：悔亡，有孚改命，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Ăn năn mất, có tin, đổi mệnh tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows occasion for repentance disappearing (from its subject). Let him be believed in; and though he change (existing) ordinances, there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九五：大人虎變，未占有孚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Người lớn cọp biến, chưa xem có tin.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the great man (producing his changes) as the tiger (does when he) changes (his stripes). Before he divines (and proceeds to action), faith has been reposed in him.

text.zh: 上六：君子豹變，小人革面，征凶，居貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Đấng quân tử beo biến, kẻ tiểu nhân đổi mặt, đi hung, ở chính.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows the superior man producing his changes as the leopard (does when he) changes (his spots), while small men change their faces (and show their obedience). To go forward (now) would lead to evil, but there will be good fortune in abiding firm and correct.

judgment.zh: 鼎：元吉，亨。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Đỉnh, cả tốt hanh.
judgment.en: Ding gives the intimation of great progress and success.

image.zh: 木上有火，鼎；君子以正位凝命。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên cây có lửa, là quẻ Đỉnh. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà chính ngôi đóng mệnh.
image.en: (The trigram representing) wood and above it that for fire form Ding. The superior man, in accordance with this, keeps his every position correct, and maintains secure the appointment (of Heaven).

text.zh: 初六：鼎顛趾，利出否，得妾以其子，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Vạc chổng chân, lợi thải bỏ vật hư xấu, được nàng hầu, lấy thửa con, không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows the caldron overthrown and its feet turned up. (But) there will be advantage in its getting rid of what was bad in it.

text.zh: 九二：鼎有實，我仇有疾，不我能即，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Vạc có cái chứa, kẻ thù ta có tật, chẳng ta hay tới, tốt.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows the caldron with the things (to be cooked) in it. (If its subject can say), 'My enemy dislikes me, but he cannot approach me,' there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：鼎耳革，其行塞，雉膏不食，方雨虧悔，終吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Tai vạc đổi, thửa đi lấp, mỡ con chim trĩ không ăn, đang mưa, hết ăn năn về thiếu, trọn tốt.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows the caldron with (the places of) its ears changed. The progress (of its subject) is (thus) stopped. The fat flesh of the pheasant (which is in the caldron) will not be eaten. But the (genial) rain will come, and the grounds for repentance will disappear. There will be good fortune in the end.

text.zh: 九四：鼎折足，覆公餗，其形渥，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Vạc gẫy chân, đổ đồ ăn của ông, thửa tội giết kín, hung.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows the caldron with its feet broken; and its contents, designed for the ruler's use, overturned and spilt. Its subject will be made to blush for shame. There will be evil.

text.zh: 六五：鼎黃耳，金鉉，利貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Vạc tai vàng, quai màu vàng, lợi về chính bền.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the caldron with yellow ears and rings of metal in them. There will be advantage through being firm and correct.

text.zh: 上九：鼎玉鉉，大吉，无不利。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Đỉnh quai ngọc, cả tốt, không gì không lợi.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows the caldron with rings of jade. There will be great good fortune, and all action taken will be in every way advantageous.

judgment.zh: 震：亨。震來虩虩，笑言啞啞。震驚百里，不喪匕鬯。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Chấn hanh, sợ lại ngơm ngớp, cười nói khanh khách, chấn kinh trăm dặm, chẳng mất môi và rượu xưởng.
judgment.en: Zhen gives the intimation of ease and development. When (the time of) movement (which it indicates) comes, (the subject of the hexagram) will be found looking out with apprehension, and yet smiling and talking cheerfully. When the movement (like a crash of thunder) terrifies all within a hundred Li, he will be (like the sincere worshipper) who is not (startled into) letting go his ladle and (cup of) sacrificial spirits.

image.zh: 洊雷，震；君子以恐懼脩省。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Liền sấm là quẻ Chấn, đấng quân tử coi đó mà sợ hãi sửa xét.
image.en: (The trigram representing) thunder, being repeated, forms Zhen. The superior man, in accordance with this, is fearful and apprehensive, cultivates (his virtue), and examines (his faults).

text.zh: 初九：震來虩虩，後笑言啞啞，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Chấn lại ngơm ngớp, sau cười nói khanh khách, tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject, when the movement approaches, looking out and around with apprehension, and afterwards smiling and talking cheerfully. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六二：震來厲，億喪貝，躋于九陵，勿逐，七日得。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Chấn lại, nguy đồ (?) mất của, lên chân chín gò, chớ đuổi, bảy ngày được.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject, when the movement approaches, in a position of peril. He judges it better to let go the articles (in his possession), and to ascend a very lofty height. There is no occasion for him to pursue after (the things he has let go); in seven days he will find them.

text.zh: 六三：震蘇蘇，震行无眚。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Chấn, thom thỏm, chấn đi, không tội.
text.en: The third six, divided, shows its subject distraught amid the startling movements going on. If those movements excite him to (right) action, there will be no mistake.

text.zh: 九四：震遂泥。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Chấn bèn đắm.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject, amid the startling movements, supinely sinking (deeper) in the mud.

text.zh: 六五：震往來厲，億无喪，有事。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Chấn đi lại, nguy, đồ (?) không mất cái có.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject going and coming amidst the startling movements (of the time), and always in peril; but perhaps he will not incur loss, and find business (which he can accomplish).

text.zh: 上六：震索索，視矍矍，征凶。震不于其躬，于其鄰，无咎。婚媾有言。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Chấn xơ xác, trông ngơ ngác, đi thì hung; chấn chẳng chưng thửa mình, chưng thửa láng giềng, không lỗi, dâu gia có điều tiếng.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject, amidst the startling movements (of the time), in breathless dismay and looking round him with trembling apprehension. If he take action, there will be evil. If, while the startling movements have not reached his own person and his neighbourhood, (he were to take precautions), there would be no error, though his relatives might (still) speak against him.

judgment.zh: 艮：艮其背，不獲其身，行其庭，不見其人，无咎。
judgment.vi: Đậu thửa lưng, chẳng được thửa mình, đi thửa sân, chẳng thấy thửa người, không lỗi.
judgment.en: When one's resting is like that of the back, and he loses all consciousness of self; when he walks in his courtyard, and does not see any (of the persons) in it,--there will be no error.

image.zh: 兼山，艮；君子以思不出其位。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gồm núi là quẻ Cấn. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà nghĩ chẳng việc thửa ngôi.
image.en: (Two trigrams representing) a mountain, one over the other, form Gen. The superior man, in accordance with this, does not go in his thoughts beyond the (duties of the) position in which he is.

text.zh: 初六：艮其趾，无咎，利永貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Đậu thửa ngón chân, không lỗi, lợi về dài lâu chính bền.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows its subject keeping his toes at rest. There will be no error; but it will be advantageous for him to be persistently firm and correct.

text.zh: 六二：艮其腓，不拯其隨，其心不快。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Đậu thửa bụng chân, chẳng cứu thửa theo, thửa lòng chẳng sướng.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject keeping the calves of his legs at rest. He cannot help (the subject of the line above) whom he follows, and is dissatisfied in his mind.

text.zh: 九三：艮其限，列其夤，厲薰心。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Đậu thửa hạn, xé thửa thăn, nguy hun lòng.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject keeping his loins at rest, and separating the ribs (from the body below). The situation is perilous, and the heart glows with suppressed excitement.

text.zh: 六四：艮其身，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Đậu thửa mình, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject keeping his trunk at rest. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六五：艮其輔，言有序，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Đậu thửa mép, nói có thứ tự, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject keeping his jawbones at rest, so that his words are (all) orderly. Occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 上九：敦艮，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Dầy đậu, tốt.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject devotedly maintaining his restfulness. There will be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 漸：女歸吉，利貞。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tiệm, con gái về, tốt, lợi về chính bền.
judgment.en: Jian suggests to us the marriage of a young lady, and the good fortune (attending it). There will be advantage in being firm and correct.

image.zh: 山上有木，漸；君子以居賢德善俗。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên núi có cây là quẻ Tiệm. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà chứa đức hiền, làm hay thói tục.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and above it that for a tree form Jian. The superior man, in accordance with this, attains to and maintains his extraordinary virtue, and makes the manners of the people good.

text.zh: 初六：鴻漸于干，小子厲，有言，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Con sếu tiến chưng bến, trẻ nhỏ nguy, có điều tiếng, không lỗi.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows the wild geese gradually approaching the shore. A young officer (in similar circumstances) will be in a position of danger, and be spoken against; but there will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：鴻漸于磐，飲食衎衎，吉。
text.vi: Con sếu tiến chưng tảng đá, ăn uống hơn hớn.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows the geese gradually approaching the large rocks, where they eat and drink joyfully and at ease. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：鴻漸于陸，夫征不復，婦孕不育，凶；利禦寇。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Con sếu tiến chưng đất liền, chồng đi chẳng lại, vợ chửa chẳng nuôi, hung, lợi về chống giặc.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows them gradually advanced to the dry plains. (It suggests also the idea of) a husband who goes on an expedition from which he does not return, and of a wife who is pregnant, but will not nourish her child. There will be evil. (The case symbolised) might be advantageous in resisting plunderers.

text.zh: 六四：鴻漸于木，或得其桷，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Con sếu tiến chưng cây, hoặc được thửa cành ngang, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth SIX, divided, shows the geese gradually advanced to the trees. They may light on the flat branches. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九五：鴻漸于陵，婦三歲不孕，終莫之勝，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Con sếu tiến chưng gò, vợ ba năm chẳng chửa, rốt lại không gì thắng nó, tốt.
text.en: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows the geese gradually advanced to the high mound. (It suggests the idea of) a wife who for three years does not become pregnant; but in the end the natural issue cannot be prevented. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：鴻漸于陸，其羽可用為儀，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Con sếu tiến chưng đường mây, lông nó khá dùng làm miều cờ, tốt.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows the geese gradually advanced to the large heights (beyond). Their feathers can be used as ornaments. There will be good fortune.

judgment.zh: 歸妹：征凶，无攸利。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Qui Muội, đi hung không thửa lợi.
judgment.en: Gui Mei indicates that (under the conditions which it denotes) action will be evil, and in no wise advantageous.

image.zh: 澤上有雷，歸妹；君子以永終知敝。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên chằm có sấm là quẻ Qui Muội. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà làm dài lâu lúc chót, biết điều hỏng.
image.en: (The trigram representing the waters of) a marsh and over it that for thunder form Gui Mei. The superior man, in accordance with this, having regard to the far-distant end, knows the mischief (that may be done at the beginning).

text.zh: 初九：歸妹以娣，跛能履，征吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Em gái về nhà chồng như vợ lẽ, quẻ biết xẻo, đi tốt.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows the younger sister married off in a position ancillary to the real wife. (It suggests the idea of) a person lame on one leg who yet manages to tramp along. Going forward will be fortunate.

text.zh: 九二：眇能視，利幽人之貞。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Chột biết trông, lợi về sự chính bền của bậc u nhân.
text.en: The second NINE, undivided, shows her blind of one eye, and yet able to see. There will be advantage in her maintaining the firm correctness of a solitary widow.

text.zh: 六三：歸妹以須，反歸以娣。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Em gái về bằng sự đợi, lại về như vợ lẽ.
text.en: The third SIX, divided, shows the younger sister who was to be married off in a mean position. She returns and accepts an ancillary position.

text.zh: 九四：歸妹愆期，遲歸有時。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Em gái về nhà chồng lỗi hẹn, chậm về, có thời.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows the younger sister who is to be married off protracting the time. She may be late in being married, but the time will come.

text.zh: 六五：帝乙歸妹，其君之袂，不如其娣之袂良，月幾望，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Vua Đế Ất gả chồng em gái, vạt áo của vua nó không đẹp bằng vạt áo của vợ lẽ nó. Mặt trăng hầu tuần vọng, tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, reminds us of the marrying of the younger sister of (king) Di-yi, when the sleeves of the princess were not equal to those of the (still) younger sister who accompanied her in an inferior capacity. (The case suggests the thought of) the moon almost full. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：女承筐无實，士刲羊无血，无攸利。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Con gái vâng giỏ, không đồ đựng; con trai giết dê, không máu; không thửa lợi.
text.en: The sixth SIX, divided, shows the young lady bearing the basket, but without anything in it, and the gentleman slaughtering the sheep, but without blood flowing from it. There will be no advantage in any way.

judgment.zh: 豐：亨，王假之，勿憂，宜日中。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Phong hanh thông, vua đến đấy, chớ lo, nên mặt trời giữa.
judgment.en: Feng intimates progress and development. When a king has reached the point (which the name denotes) there is no occasion to be anxious (through fear of a change). Let him be as the sun at noon.

image.zh: 雷電皆至，豐；君子以折獄致刑。
image.vi: Sấm chớp đều đến là quẻ Phong. Đấng quân tử coi mà bẻ ngục đến hình.
image.en: (The trigrams representing) thunder and lightning combine to form Feng. The superior man, in accordance with this, decides cases of litigation, and apportions punishments with exactness.

text.zh: 初九：遇其配主，雖旬无咎，往有尚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Gặp thử chủ sánh, đều không lỗi, đi có chuộng.
text.en: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject meeting with his mate. Though they are both of the same character, there will be no error. Advance will call forth approval.

text.zh: 六二：豐其蔀，日中見斗，往得疑疾，有孚發若，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Thịnh thửa trướng che, giữa ban ngày thấy sao Đẩu, đi thì găp ngờ ghét, có tin dường mở ra vậy, tốt.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows its subject surrounded by screens so large and thick that at midday he can see from them the constellation of the Bushel. If he go (and try to enlighten his ruler who is thus emblemed), he will make himself to be viewed with suspicion and dislike. Let him cherish his feeling of sincere devotion that he may thereby move (his ruler's mind), and there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九三：豐其沛，日中見沬，折其右肱，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Thịnh thửa màn, giữa ban ngày thấy sao Mạt, gãy thửa cánh tay phải, không lỗi.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject with an (additional) screen of a large and thick banner, through which at midday he can see (the small) Mei star. (In the darkness) he breaks his right arm; but there will be no error.

text.zh: 九四：豐其蔀，日中見斗，遇其夷主，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Thịnh thửa trướng, giữa ban ngày thấy sao Đẩu, gặp thửa chủ ngang, tốt.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject in a tent so large and thick that at midday he can see from it the constellation of the Bushel. But he meets with the subject of the (first) line, undivided like himself. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六五：來章，有慶譽，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Lại đẹp có phúc khen, tốt.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject bringing around him the men of brilliant ability. There will be occasion for congratulation and praise. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上六：豐其屋，蔀其家，闚其戶，闃其无人，三歲不覿，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Thịnh thửa mái, che thửa nhà nhòm thửa cửa, hiu quạnh thửa không người, ba năm chẳng thấy, hung.
text.en: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject with his house made large, but only serving as a screen to his household. When he looks at his door, it is still, and there is nobody about it. For three years no one is to be seen. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 旅：小亨，旅貞吉。
judgment.vi: Quẻ Lữ, hanh thông nhỏ, đi đường chính bền tốt.
judgment.en: Lu intimates that (in the condition which it denotes) there may be some little attainment and progress. If the stranger or traveller be firm and correct as he ought to be, there will be good fortune.

image.zh: 山上有火，旅；君子以明慎用刑，而不留獄。
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên núi có lửa là quẻ Lữ, đấng quân tử coi đó mà sáng ghín dùng hình, mà chẳng để đọng ngục.
image.en: (The trigram representing) a mountain and above it that for fire form Lu. The superior man, in accordance with this, exerts his wisdom and caution in the use of punishments and not allowing litigations to continue.

text.zh: 旅瑣瑣，斯其所取災。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Hành lữ nhỏ mọn, ấy là cái lấy vạ.
text.en: The first SIX, divided, shows the stranger mean and meanly occupied. It is thus that he brings on himself (further) calamity.

text.zh: 旅即次，懷其資，得童僕貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Hành lữ tới chỗ trọ, ôm thửa của, được thằng nhỏ đầy tớ chính bền.
text.en: The second SIX, divided, shows the stranger, occupying his lodging-house, carrying with him his means of livelihood, and provided with good and trusty servants.

text.zh: 旅焚其次，喪其童僕，貞厲。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Hành lữ cháy thửa nhà trọ, mất thửa thằng nhỏ đầy tớ, chính bền nguy.
text.en: The third NINE, undivided, shows the stranger, burning his lodging-house, and having lost his servants. However firm and correct he (try to) be, he will be in peril.

text.zh: 旅于處，得其資斧，我心不快。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Hành lữ chưng ở, được thửa của búa, lòng ta chẳng sướng.
text.en: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows the traveller in a resting-place, having (also) the means of livelihood and the axe, (but still saying), 'I am not at ease in my mind.'

text.zh: 射雉一矢亡，終以譽命。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Bắn chim trĩ, một phát tên mất, chót được khen mệnh.
text.en: The fifth SIX, divided, shows its subject shooting a pheasant. He will lose his arrow, but in the end he will obtain praise and a (high) charge.

text.zh: 鳥焚其巢，旅人先笑後號咷。喪牛于易，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Chim cháy thửa tổ, kẻ hành lữ trước cười, sau kêu gào, mất trâu dễ dàng, hung.
text.en: The sixth NINE, undivided, suggests the idea of a bird burning its nest. The stranger, (thus represented), first laughs and then cries out. He has lost his ox(-like docility) too readily and easily. There will be evil.

judgment.zh: 巽：小亨。利有攸往。利見大人。
judgment.en: Xun intimates that (under the conditions which it denotes) there will be some little attainment and progress. There will be advantage in movement onward in whatever direction. It will be advantageous (also) to see the great man.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tốn nhỏ hanh thông, lợi có thửa đi, lời thấy người lớn.

image.zh: 隨風，巽；君子以申命行事。
image.en: (Two trigrams representing) wind, following each other, form Xun. The superior man, in accordance with this, reiterates his orders, and secures the practice of his affairs.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Theo gió là quẻ Tốn. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà nhắc lại mệnh lệnh, làm việc.

text.zh: 初六：進退，利武人之貞。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Tiến lui, lợi về sự chính bền của người võ.
text.en: The first line, divided, shows its subject (now) advancing, (now) receding. It would be advantageous for him to have the firm correctness of a brave soldier.

text.zh: 九二：巽在牀下，用史巫紛若，吉无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Nhún ở dưới giường, dùng thày bói, thày cúng bời bời vậy, tốt, không lỗi.
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows the representative of Xun beneath a couch, and employing diviners and exorcists in a way bordering on confusion. There will be good fortune and no error.

text.zh: 九三：頻巽，吝。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Liền nhún, đáng tiếc.
text.en: The third line, undivided, shows its subject penetrating (only) by violent and repeated efforts. There will be occasion for regret.

text.zh: 六四：悔亡，田獲三品。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Ăn năn mất, săn được ba phẩm.
text.en: The fourth line, divided, shows all occasion for repentance (in its subject) passed away. He takes game for its threefold use in his hunting.

text.zh: 九五：貞吉悔亡，无不利。无初有終，先庚三日，後庚三日，吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Chính bền tốt, ăn năn mất, không gì lợi, không đầu có cuối, trước Canh ba ngày, sau Canh ba ngày, tốt.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows that with firm correctness there will be good fortune (to its subject). All occasion for repentance will disappear, and all his movements will be advantageous. There may have been no (good) beginning, but there will be a (good) end. Three days before making any changes, (let him give notice of them); and three days after, (let him reconsider them). There will (thus) be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：巽在牀下，喪其資斧，貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Nhún ở dưới giường, mất thửa của búa, hung.
text.en: The sixth line, undivided, shows the representative of penetration beneath a couch, and having lost the axe with which he executed his decisions. However firm and correct he may (try to) be, there will be evil.

judgment.zh: 兌：亨。利貞。
judgment.en: Dui intimates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and attainment. (But) it will be advantageous to be firm and correct.

image.zh: 麗澤，兌；君子以朋友講習。
image.en: (Two symbols representing) the waters of a marsh, one over the other, form Dui. The superior man, in accordance with this, (encourages) the conversation of friends and (the stimulus of) their (common) practice.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Bám chằm là quẻ Đoái, đấng quân tử coi đó mà bè bạn giảng tập.

text.zh: 初九：和兌，吉。
text.en: The first line, undivided, shows the pleasure of (inward) harmony. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九二：孚兌，吉，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Tin đẹp lòng, tốt, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows the pleasure arising from (inward) sincerity. There will be good fortune. Occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 六三：來兌，凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Lại làm đẹp lòng, hung.
text.en: The third line, divided, shows its subject bringing round himself whatever can give pleasure. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九四：商兌，未寧，介疾有喜。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Đắn đo đẹp lòng chưa yên, thẳng ghét, có mừng.
text.en: The fourth line, undivided, shows its subject deliberating about what to seek his pleasure in, and not at rest. He borders on what would be injurious, but there will be cause for joy.

text.zh: 九五：孚于剝，有厲。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Tin chung đẽo, có nguy.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows its subject trusting in one who would injure him. The situation is perilous.

text.zh: 上六：引兌。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Kéo dài sự đẹp lòng.
text.en: The topmost line, divided, shows the pleasure of its subject in leading and attracting others.

judgment.zh: 渙：亨。王假有廟，利涉大川，利貞。
judgment.en: Huan intimates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and success. The king goes to his ancestral temple; and it will be advantageous to cross the great stream. It will be advantageous to be firm and correct.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Hoán hanh, vua đến có miếu, lợi sang sông lớn, lợi về chính bền.

image.zh: 風行水上，渙；先王以享于帝立廟。
image.en: (The trigram representing) water and that for wind moving above the water form Huan. The ancient kings, in accordance with this, presented offerings to God and established the ancestral temple.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Gió đi trên nước là quẻ Hoán. Đấng tiên vương coi đó mà cúng trời, dựng miếu.

text.zh: 初六：用拯馬壯，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Vớt ngựa mạnh, tốt.
text.en: The first line, divided, shows its subject engaged in rescuing (from the impending evil) and having (the assistance of) a strong horse. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 九二：渙奔其机，悔亡。
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows its subject, amid the dispersion, hurrying to his contrivance (for security). All occasion for repentance will disappear.

text.zh: 六三：渙其躬，无悔。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Tan thửa mình, không ăn năn.
text.en: The third line, divided, shows its subject discarding any regard to his own person. There will be no occasion for repentance.

text.zh: 六四：渙其群，元吉。渙有丘，匪夷所思。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Tan thửa đàn, cả tốt; tan có gò, chẳng phải thường thửa nghĩ.
text.en: The fourth line, divided, shows its subject scattering the (different) parties (in the state); which leads to great good fortune. From the dispersion (he collects again good men standing out, a crowd) like a mound, which is what ordinary men would not have thought of.

text.zh: 九五：渙汗其大號，渙王居，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Tan bồ hôi thửa hiệu lớn, tan chỗ ở của vua, không lỗi.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows its subject amidst the dispersion issuing his great announcements as the perspiration (flows from his body). He scatters abroad (also) the accumulations in the royal granaries. There will be no error.

text.zh: 上九：渙其血，去逖出，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Tan thửa máu đi, rùng rợn ra, không lỗi.
text.en: The topmost line, undivided, shows its subject disposing of (what may be called) its bloody wounds, and going and separating himself from its anxious fears. There will be no error.

judgment.zh: 節：亨。苦節不可貞。
judgment.en: Jie intimates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and attainment. (But) if the regulations (which it prescribes) be severe and difficult, they cannot be permanent.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tiết hanh, sự dè dặt khổ không thể chính bền.

image.zh: 澤上有水，節；君子以制數度，議德行。
image.en: (The trigram representing) a lake, and above it that for water, form Jie. The superior man, in accordance with this, constructs his (methods of) numbering and measurement, and discusses (points of) virtue and conduct.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên chằm có nước là quẻ Tiết. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà chế độ, bàn đức nết.

text.zh: 初九：不出戶庭，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Chẳng ra sân cửa, không lỗi.
text.en: The first line, undivided, shows its subject not quitting the courtyard outside his door. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九二：不出門庭，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Chẳng ra sân cổng, hung.
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows its subject not quitting the courtyard inside his gate. There will be evil.

text.zh: 六三：不節若，則嗟若，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Chẳng dè dặt vậy thì than thở vậy, không lỗi.
text.en: The third line, divided, shows its subject with no appearance of observing the (proper) regulations, in which case we shall see him lamenting. But there will be no one to blame (but himself).

text.zh: 六四：安節，亨。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Yên dè dặt, hanh.
text.en: The fourth line, divided, shows its subject quietly and naturally (attentive to all) regulations. There will be progress and success.

text.zh: 九五：甘節，吉；往有尚。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Sự dè dặt ngọt, tốt, đi có chuộng.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows its subject sweetly and acceptably enacting his regulations. There will be good fortune. The onward progress with them will afford ground for admiration.

text.zh: 上六：苦節，貞凶，悔亡。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Sự dè dặt khổ, chính bền hung, ăn năn mất.
text.en: The topmost line, divided, shows its subject enacting regulations severe and difficult. Even with firmness and correctness there will be evil. But though there will be cause for repentance, it will (by and by) disappear.

judgment.zh: 中孚：豚魚吉，利涉大川，利貞。
judgment.en: Zhong Fu (moves even) pigs and fish, and leads to good fortune. There will be advantage in crossing the great stream. There will be advantage in being firm and correct.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Trung Phu: Cá lợn tốt, lợi sang sông lớn, lợi về chính bền.

image.zh: 澤上有風，中孚；君子以議獄緩死。
image.en: (The trigram representing the waters of) a marsh and that for wind above it form Zhong Fu. The superior man, in accordance with this, deliberates about cases of litigation and delays (the infliction of) death.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên chằm có gió là quẻ Trung Phu. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà bàn việc ngục, hoãn tội chết.

text.zh: 初九：虞吉，有他不燕。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Lo tốt, có khác, chẳng yên.
text.en: The first line, undivided, shows its subject resting (in himself). There will be good fortune. If he sought to any other, he would not find rest.

text.zh: 九二：鳴鶴在陰，其子和之，我有好爵，吾與爾靡之。
text.vi: Hào Chín Hai: Con Hạc kêu ở chỗ tối, con nó họa đấy; ta có tước tốt, tao cùng mày thích đấy.
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows its subject (like) the crane crying out in her hidden retirement, and her young ones responding to her. (It is as if it were said), 'I have a cup of good spirits,' (and the response were), 'I will partake of it with you.'

text.zh: 六三：得敵，或鼓或罷，或泣或歌。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Ba: Được kẻ địch, hoặc khua trống hoặc thôi, hoặc khóc hoặc múa.
text.en: The third line, divided, shows its subject having met with his mate. Now he beats his drum, and now he leaves off. Now he weeps, and now he sings.

text.zh: 六四：月幾望，馬匹亡，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: mặt trăng hầu tuần vọng, đôi ngựa mất, không lỗi.
text.en: The fourth line, divided, shows its subject (like) the moon nearly full, and (like) a horse (in a chariot) whose fellow disappears. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九五：有孚攣如，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Có tin dường như co quắp vậy, không lỗi.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows its subject perfectly sincere, and linking (others) to him in closest union. There will be no error.

text.zh: 上九：翰音登于天，貞凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: Tiếng cánh lên chưng trời, chính bền hung.
text.en: The topmost line, undivided, shows its subject in chanticleer (trying to) mount to heaven. Even with firm correctness there will be evil.

judgment.zh: 小過：亨。利貞。可小事，不可大事。飛鳥遺之音，不宜上宜下，大吉。
judgment.en: Xiao Guo indicates that (in the circumstances which it implies) there will be progress and attainment. But it will be advantageous to be firm and correct. (What the name denotes), may be done in small affairs, but not in great affairs. (It is like) the notes that come down from a bird on the wing;—to descend is better than to ascend. There will (in this way) be great good fortune.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Tiểu Quá hanh, lợi về chính bền. Khá việc nhỏ, chẳng khá việc lớn, chim bay để chưng tiếng, chẳng nên lên, nên xuống, cả tốt.

image.zh: 山上有雷，小過；君子以行過乎恭，喪過乎哀，用過乎儉。
image.en: (The trigram representing) a hill and that for thunder above it form Xiao Guo. The superior man, in accordance with this, in his conduct exceeds in humility, in mourning exceeds in sorrow, and in his expenditure exceeds in economy.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Trên núi có sấm là quẻ Tiểu Quá. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà nên nết quá chưng kính, tang quá chưng thương, dùng quá chưng kiệm.

text.zh: 初六：飛鳥以凶。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Chim bay là hung.
text.en: The first line, divided, suggests (the idea of) a bird flying, (and ascending) till the issue is evil.

text.zh: 六二：過其祖，遇其妣；不及其君，遇其臣；无咎。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Quá thửa ông, gặp thửa bà; chẳng kịp thửa vua, gặp thửa bề tôi, không lỗi.
text.en: The second line, divided, shows its subject passing by his grandfather, and meeting with his grandmother; not attempting anything against his ruler, but meeting him as his minister. There will be no error.

text.zh: 九三：弗過防之，從或戕之，凶。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Chẳng quá ngừa đó, theo hoặc hại đó, hung.
text.en: The third line, undivided, shows its subject taking no extraordinary precautions against danger; and some in consequence finding opportunity to assail and injure him. There will be evil.

text.zh: 九四：无咎，弗過遇之。往厲必戒，勿用永貞。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tự: Không lỗi chẳng quá gặp đấy, đi nguy, ắt răn, chớ dùng dài lâu chính bền.
text.en: The fourth line, undivided, shows its subject falling into no error, but meeting (the exigency of his situation), without exceeding (in his natural course). If he go forward, there will be peril, and he must be cautious. There is no occasion to be using firmness perpetually.

text.zh: 六五：密雲不雨，自我西郊，公弋取彼在穴。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Mây dầy chẳng mưa, ở cõi Tây lại, ông bắn lấy nó ở hang.
text.en: The fifth line, divided, (suggests the idea) of dense clouds, but no rain, coming from our borders in the west. It also (shows) the prince shooting his arrow, and taking the bird in a cave.

text.zh: 上六：弗遇過之，飛鳥離之，凶，是謂災眚。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Chẳng gặp, quá đấy, chim bay lìa đấy, hung, ấy rằng vạ tội.
text.en: The sixth line, divided, shows its subject not meeting (the exigency of his situation), and exceeding (his proper course). (It suggests the idea of) a bird flying far aloft. There will be evil. The case is what is called one of calamity and self-produced injury.

judgment.zh: 既濟：亨小。利貞。初吉終亂。
judgment.en: Ji Ji intimates progress and success in small matters. There will be advantage in being firm and correct. There has been good fortune in the beginning; there may be disorder in the end.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Ký Tế hanh, lợi nhỏ về chính bền, đầu tốt, chót loạn.

image.zh: 水在火上，既濟；君子以思患而豫防之。
image.en: (The trigram representing) fire and that for water above it form Ji Ji. The superior man, in accordance with this, thinks of evil (that may come), and beforehand guards against it.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Nước ở trên lửa là quẻ Ký Tế. Đấng quân tử coi đó để nghĩ sự lo mà ngừa sẵn đấy.

text.zh: 初九：曳其輪，濡其尾，无咎。
text.vi: Hào Chín Đầu: Kéo thửa bánh xe, ướt thửa đuôi không lỗi.
text.en: The first line, undivided, (shows its subject as a driver) who drags back his wheel, (or as a fox) which has wet his tail. There will be no error.

text.zh: 六二：婦喪其茀，勿逐，七日得。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Hai: Đàn bà mất thửa khăn trùm chớ đuổi, bảy ngày được.
text.en: The second line, divided, (shows its subject as) a wife who has lost her (carriage-)screen. There is no occasion to go in pursuit of it. In seven days she will find it.

text.zh: 九三：高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用。
text.vi: Hào Chín Ba: Vua Cao Tông đánh nước Quỷ Phương, ba năm được đấy, kẻ tiểu nhân chờ dùng.
text.en: The third line, undivided, (suggests the case of) Gao Zong who attacked the Demon region, but was three years in subduing it. Small men should not be employed (in such enterprises).

text.zh: 六四：繻有衣袽，終日戒。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Tư: Ướt đã có áo giẻ, trọn ngày răn.
text.en: The fourth line, divided, shows its subject with rags provided against any leak (in his boat), and on his guard all day long.

text.zh: 九五：東鄰殺牛，不如西鄰之禴祭，實受其福。
text.vi: Hào Chín Năm: Láng giềng bên Đông giết trâu chẳng bằng láng giềng bên Tây tế Thược, thật chịu thửa phúc.
text.en: The fifth line, undivided, shows its subject (as) the neighbour in the east who slaughters an ox (for his sacrifice); but this is not equal to the (small) spring sacrifice of the neighbour in the west, whose sincerity receives the blessing.

text.zh: 上六：濡其首，厲。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Trên: Ướt thửa đầu, nguy!
text.en: The topmost line, divided, shows its subject with (even) his head immersed. The position is perilous.

judgment.zh: 未濟：亨。小狐汔濟，濡其尾，无攸利。
judgment.en: Wei Ji intimates progress and success (in the circumstances which it implies). (We see) a young fox that has nearly crossed (the stream), when its tail gets immersed. There will be no advantage in any way.
judgment.vi: Quẻ Vị Tế hanh, con cáo nhỏ hầu sang, ướt thửa đuôi, không thửa lợi.

image.zh: 火在水上，未濟；君子以慎辨物居方。
image.en: (The trigram representing) water and that for fire above it form Wei Ji. The superior man, in accordance with this, carefully discriminates among (the qualities of) things, and the (different) positions they (naturally) occupy.
image.vi: Lời Tượng nói rằng: Lửa ở trên nước, là quẻ Vị Tế. Đấng quân tử coi đó mà cẩn thận phân biệt các vật, ở phương.

text.zh: 初六：濡其尾，吝。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Đầu: Ướt thửa đuôi, đáng thẹn.
text.en: The first line, divided, shows its subject (like a fox) whose tail gets immersed. There will be occasion for regret.

text.zh: 九二：曳其輪，貞吉。
text.vi: Hào Chín hai: Kéo thửa bánh xe, chính bền, tốt.
text.en: The second line, undivided, shows its subject dragging back his (carriage-)wheel. With firmness and correctness there will be good fortune.

text.zh: 六三：未濟，征凶，利涉大川。
text.vi: Hào Sáu ba: Chưa sang, đi hung, lợi về sang sông lớn.
text.en: The third line, divided, shows its subject, with (the state of things) not yet remedied, advancing on; which will lead to evil. But there will be advantage in (trying to) cross the great stream.

text.zh: 九四：貞吉，悔亡，震用伐鬼方，三年有賞于大國。
text.vi: Hào Chín Tư: Chính bền, ăn năn mất, chấn dùng đánh nước Quỷ Phương, ba năm có thưởng chưng nước lớn.
text.en: The fourth line, undivided, shows its subject by firm correctness obtaining good fortune, so that all occasion for repentance disappears. Let him stir himself up, as if he were invading the Demon region, where for three years rewards will come to him (and his troops) from the great kingdom.

text.zh: 六五：貞吉，无悔，君子之光，有孚，吉。
text.vi: Hào Sáu Năm: Chính bền tốt, không ăn năn, sự sáng của đấng quân tử, có tin tốt.
text.en: The fifth line, divided, shows its subject by firm correctness obtaining good fortune, and having no occasion for repentance. (We see in him) the brightness of a superior man, and the possession of sincerity. There will be good fortune.

text.zh: 上九：有孚于飲酒，无咎，濡其首，有孚失是。
text.vi: Hào Chín Trên: có tin chưng uống rượu, không lỗi, ướt thửa đầu, có tin, đúng là mất.
text.en: The topmost line, undivided, shows its subject drinking his wine, full of confidence and therefore feasting (quietly). There will be no error. 'He drinks and gets his head immersed:'—he does not know how to submit to the (proper) regulations.
